Flip the bun bottoms and the hot dogs together onto a cutting board. Top with SLICED CANNED PICKLED JALAPEÑOS, THINLY SLICED WHITE ONIONS, slices of RIPE MEXICAN HASS AVOCADO, and a pinch of salt. Cover with the roll tops and press firmly but gently.

tortellini-alla-panna-traditional-recipe.name: Tortellini Alla Panna (traditional Recipe With Cream And Ham)
tortellini-alla-panna-traditional-recipe.original_name: Tortellini alla Panna (receta tradicional con nata y jamón)
instructions: Mientras los tortellini se cuecen, en una cacerola amplia fundimos la mantequilla a fuego suave.

When Fold we have put some seconds of ham and we can add peas to the pits to make the plate more complete. We add cream, nutmeg, salt and pepper we mix everything and let them get a taste 1 or 2 minutes.
Apagamos el fuego, echamos los tortellini cocidos en la cacerola y mezclamos Servimos con queso parmesano rallado por encima.

tortellini-gratinata-with-mushrooms-and-.name: Tortellini Gratinata With Mushrooms And Parsnip "béchamel

ingredients.item: - to 9-ounce packages cheese-filled tortellini or tortelloni

instructions: Cook parsnips in medium saucepan of boiling salted water until very tender, about 20 minutes. Drain; reserve saucepan. Transfer parsnips to processor. Add 11/2 cups milk. Blend until smooth. With machine running, gradually add remaining 1 cup milk through feed tube. Add 3/4 cup Parmesan cheese and nutmeg; blend well. Return sauce to reserved pan. Simmer over low heat until reduced to 3 cups, whisking often, about 5 minutes. Season béchamel sauce to taste with salt and freshly ground black pepper.

Melt 21/2 tablespoons butter in heavy large skillet over medium-high heat. Add mushrooms, garlic, and rosemary. Sauté until mushrooms are brown and tender, 6 to 7 minutes. Season to taste with salt and pepper. do ahead Béchamel sauce and mushrooms DO AHEADCan be made 1 day ahead. Cool, cover separately, and chill. Rewarm béchamel sauce before using.

Preheat oven to 204°C. Butter 12-cup-capacity baking dish. Cook pasta in large pot of boiling salted water until just tender but still firm to bite, stirring occasionally. Drain pasta; return to pot. Add remaining 2 tablespoons butter and toss to coat. Stir in mushroom mixture. Transfer pasta to prepared baking dish. Spread béchamel sauce over; sprinkle with Gorgonzola, then remaining 1/4 cup Parmesan.
Bake pasta until heated through and sauce is bubbling, 18 to 20 minutes. If desired, broil until top is golden, about 2 minutes. Let stand 10 minutes and serve.

tortellini-in-aromatic-sauce-with-mozzar.name: Tortellini In Aromatic Sauce With Mozzarella
tortellini-in-aromatic-sauce-with-mozzar.original_name: Τορτελίνια σε αρωματική σάλτσα με μοτσαρέλα

ingredients.item: 250 gromotsarella cut into small cubes (or messenian talagani or halumi Cypriot -always a mastic cheese)
250 gromotsarella cut into small cubes (or messenian talagani or halumi Cypriot -always a mastic cheese)

instructions: Start the tortellini, sautéing in olive oil in a pot the garlic for 2 minutes.
Add the tomatoes, herbs, salt, pepper and sugar.
Simmer the sauce over low heat for 45 minutes, to bind.
Meanwhile, boil the tortellines and drain them.
Add them to the aromatic tomato sauce, rub pepper and sprinkle the parmesan.
We mix very well.
Remove from heat and add the mozzarella cubes.
Mix and serve on a deep platter.
If we want to garnish with finely chopped mint and basil.

tortellini-in-chicken-broth.name: Tortellini In Chicken Broth

ingredients.item: Freshly ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Stir broth and water together in a pot; bring to a boil.

Stir frozen tortellini into broth mixture and cook, occasionally stirring, until tortellini floats to the top and filling is hot, 6 to 8 minutes. Stir watercress and scallions into broth mixture and cook until watercress wilts, about 2 minutes. Add hot pepper sauce.
Sprinkle with Romano cheese and black pepper to serve.

tortellini-in-preserved-lemon-brodo.name: Tortellini In Preserved Lemon Brodo
instructions: Melt ¼ cup (½ stick/55 g) unsalted butter in a large pot over medium heat. Lay 1 small preserved lemon, thinly sliced, seeds removed, across the bottom in an even layer. Cook until the butter begins to smell nutty and starts to brown, 3–5 minutes. Add the 3 garlic cloves, minced, stir to combine, and cook until fragrant and golden around the edges, another 2 minutes or so. Add 8 cups (1.9 L) drinkable chicken broth and stir to combine.
Increase the heat to high and bring to a boil. Add 1¼ lb. (570 g) fresh cheese tortellini and cook in the broth according to the package directions, usually 3–5 minutes.
Divide the tortellini among bowls, pour over the broth, and serve with freshly cracked black pepper, a heap of grated Parmesan cheese, some lemon zest, and a squeeze of lemon.

tortellini-in-the-oven-argiro.name: Tortellini In The Oven

ingredients.item: 1/2 cup of water boiled by pasta
200 gritish gouda grated (or other melted yellow cheese)
1/2 cup of water boiled by pasta
200 gritish gouda grated (or other melted yellow cheese)

instructions: Boil the tortellines in salted water for 10′ to be al dente.
Drain and keep 1/2 cup of pasta water aside.
Add the tortellini to a pyrex and sprinkle with the olive oil and mix them to oil.
Add the bacon chopped.
Mix the cream with water and parmesan and pour the tortellines.
Sprinkle with some nutmeg and some pepper, make a layer of grated cheese on the tortellines.
Bake the tortellines in the oven at 180°C in the air on the medium grill in a well preheated oven for about 20′ until the cheese melts and becomes a thick sauce along with the cream.

Tortellins in the oven must be served immediately. Otherwise they'll pull all the moisture in the baking pan, dry and harden. The melted cheese with the cream turns into cheese cream that is as warm as it is fluid but when cold it tightens a lot.
Boil the tortellini always in salted water and 2-3′ less than the package. So when they get in the oven they'll be al-dente and by baking they'll come to the desired stage.

tortellini-primavera.name: Tortellini Primavera
instructions: Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook tortellini in boiling water until tender yet firm to the bite and filling is warmed through, 5 to 7 minutes; drain.

Combine cooked tortellini, tomatoes, broccoli, yellow bell pepper, Parmesan cheese, and pepperoni in a large serving bowl. Pour in 1 cup Italian dressing and toss to coat. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 4 hours. If salad looks dry, stir in remaining 1/4 cup Italian dressing before serving.

tortellini-salad.name: Tortellini Salad
instructions: Fill a large pot with lightly salted water and bring to a rolling boil. Stir in spinach and three-cheese tortellini and return to a boil. Cook, uncovered, stirring occasionally, until tortellini float to the top, pasta tender yet firm to the bite, and filling is hot, 10 to 11 minutes. Drain well; cool tortellini.
Combine tortellini, grape tomatoes, artichoke hearts, olives, red onion, and carrots in a large bowl; toss with dressing.
Cover bowl with plastic wrap; refrigerate until chilled, at least 3 hours to overnight.

tortellini-salad-with-tomatoes-and-peas.name: Tortellini Salad With Tomatoes And Peas
instructions: Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook tortellini in the boiling water until tender yet firm to the bite, 8 to 10 minutes. Drain; set aside to cool.
Meanwhile, bring a saucepan of water to a boil; cook peas in the boiling water for 2 minutes. Drain; set aside to cool.
Combine cooled tortellini, cooled peas, tomatoes, Parmesan cheese, chives, and parsley in a large bowl.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, sugar, ¾ teaspoon salt, and ⅛ teaspoon black pepper together in a small bowl until well combined; it will taste sour. Pour vinaigrette over tortellini salad; toss well.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap; refrigerate at least 2 hours. Season with salt and black pepper before serving.

tortellini-soup.name: Tortellini Soup
instructions: Melt the olive oil and butter together in a Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the onion, garlic, red pepper flakes, salt, and black pepper. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the onions are translucent and the garlic is fragrant, about 5 minutes.
Add the white wine and cook for another 2 minutes, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot with a wooden spoon, until the wine is reduced by at least half.
Add the vegetable broth, crushed tomatoes, and bay leaf. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add the tortellini and spinach and simmer for another 5–6 minutes, until the tortellini are cooked through and the spinach has wilted.
Remove the bay leaf, then stir in the heavy cream.
Ladle the soup into bowls and garnish with the Parmesan cheese and basil.
Enjoy!

tortellini-with-brown-butter-and-sage.name: Tortellini With Brown Butter And Sage
instructions: Bring a large pot of well-salted water to a boil. Meanwhile, do some prep. First, chop ¾ cup walnuts so they’re roughly the size of peas.
Smash 5 garlic cloves, peel, and discard skins. (You don’t want the cloves to be obliterated, but smashed enough create plenty of surface area.)
Cut ½ cup (1 stick) unsalted butter into 8 pieces and transfer to a large skillet. Heat skillet over medium-low and cook butter, swirling often, until just melted, 2–3 minutes.

Add walnuts and garlic and continue to cook, stirring often with a heatproof spatula, until nuts and garlic are light golden brown and butter solids are browned as well, 3–4 minutes longer. It will get very foamy and hard to see the bottom of the skillet, so use spatula to drag through butter—you should leave a trail of little brown specks in your wake (a.k.a. toasty, delicious milk solids).

Add 16 sage leaves and cook (still over medium-low heat), stirring constantly, until sage crackles and is dark green and crisp, about 1 minute longer. Remove skillet from heat. Hot tip: If you think things are getting too dark, add a splash or two of pasta cooking liquid to the hot pan; this will halt the browning process. Season with 1½ tsp. salt and ½ tsp. pepper.

Cook 1 lb. store-bought cheese tortellini (or other stuffed pasta) in boiling salted water and set a timer according to package directions, but start checking doneness a minute early. Fresh pasta packages are often a little generous with their cook times, so it pays to check sooner rather than later—you don't want falling-apart pasta! Stir pasta once or twice as it cooks to ensure it cooks evenly.

When pasta is cooked, drain and transfer to butter sauce. Return skillet to medium heat and cook, stirring and tossing, until pasta is well coated in sauce, about 30 seconds. Remove from heat and stir in 2 tsp. apple cider vinegar. Taste sauce and add more salt if needed.
Divide tortellini among plates. Sprinkle with 2 oz. Parmesan and a few good cranks of pepper.

tortellini-with-garlicky-brown-butter-an.name: Tortellini With Garlicky Brown Butter And Dill
instructions: Cook 4 oz. fresh cheese tortellini (about 1 heaping cup) in a large pot of boiling salted water, stirring occasionally, until very al dente, about 2 minutes less than package directions.

Meanwhile, cook 2 Tbsp. unsalted butter, cut into pieces, in a medium skillet over medium heat until golden brown and nutty smelling, 2–3 minutes (watch carefully). Remove from heat and stir in 2 garlic cloves, finely chopped, a pinch of salt, and several grinds of freshly ground pepper.

Using a mesh sieve, scoop tortellini out of cooking liquid and transfer to skillet with brown butter sauce. Set over low heat and toss to coat, adding a splash or two of pasta cooking liquid if you’d like a looser sauce. Stir in ¼ cup chopped dill and juice of ½ lemon.
Transfer pasta to a shallow bowl and top with zest of ½ lemon; season with flaky sea salt and more pepper.

tortellini-with-gorgonzola-lezzet.name: Tortellini With Gorgonzola
instructions: 1. pürüzsüzsüzed dough knead.
2. Streç yellow to the film and leave at least 30 minutes to rest.
3. Walnuts, aroma in the pan &e; roast up to the pan.
4. Prepare a mild syrup to boil sugar and juice.
5.  Soyed peudu this syrup's iç take the edge of the iç soak and cool until softened.
6. Küçü heat in jealous fire in a sauce pot.
7. Add the cheese with Gorgonzola and mix it until soon.
8. Cool the mixture from the filler consistency into the light-and-light.
9. Brief s&uuml in the pan of pecan walnuts;re roasting and flavoring &gray;free.
10.  Dined pulp thinning açin.
11. 11. Ü küçük parçput Gorgonzola filler in the form of the people.
12. Cover the other dough with solid and press the edges and form the tortellini.
12. August Prepared tortellini’s plenty of salty boiling water in consistency.
14 July  In a pan, melt the butter and add it to the lemon peel.
15. In addition to some pasta juice, prepare emülsion.
16. Boiled tortellini’
17. September Place walnut cream sürüpütortellini’s on the bottom of the service plate.
18. Serve s&uuml with prepared pear slices and roasted pecan walnuts.

tortellini-with-italian-sausage-fennel-a.name: Tortellini With Italian Sausage, Fennel, And Mushroom

ingredients.prep: trimmed, halved through core, thinly sliced lengthwise (about 3 cups), fronds chopped

ingredients.item: Package sliced fresh crimini (baby bella) mushrooms
Package dried tortellini with pesto filling or fresh tortellini with 3-cheese filling
Finely grated parmesan cheese plus additional

instructions: Heat oil in large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add sliced fennel bulb, sausage, and mushrooms; sauté until sausage is brown and cooked through and fennel is almost tender, 12 to 15 minutes. Add garlic and fennel seeds; stir 1 minute. Stir in cream, then 1 cup broth; boil until liquid is reduced and very slightly thickened, 2 to 3 minutes.
Meanwhile, cook tortellini in large pot of boiling salted water until just tender but still firm to bite, stirring occasionally. Drain tortellini; return to same pot.

Add sausage mixture to tortellini in pot. Toss over medium heat until blended. Add spinach; toss gently until spinach wilts. Stir in ½ cup cheese; add more broth by ¼ cupfuls to moisten if dry. Season with salt and pepper, sprinkle with chopped fennel fronds, and serve, passing additional cheese.

tortellini-with-italian-sausage-fennel-a.name: Tortellini With Italian Sausage, Fennel, And Mushrooms

ingredients.prep: trimmed, halved through core, thinly sliced lengthwise (about 3 cups), fronds chopped

ingredients.item: Package sliced fresh crimini (baby bella) mushrooms
Package dried tortellini with pesto filling or fresh tortellini with 3-cheese filling
Finely grated parmesan cheese plus additional

instructions: Heat oil in large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add sliced fennel bulb, sausage, and mushrooms; sauté until sausage is brown and cooked through and fennel is almost tender, 12 to 15 minutes. Add garlic and fennel seeds; stir 1 minute. Stir in cream, then 1 cup broth; boil until liquid is reduced and very slightly thickened, 2 to 3 minutes.
Meanwhile, cook tortellini in large pot of boiling salted water until just tender but still firm to bite, stirring occasionally. Drain tortellini; return to same pot.

Add sausage mixture to tortellini in pot. Toss over medium heat until blended. Add spinach; toss gently until spinach wilts. Stir in ½ cup cheese; add more broth by ¼ cupfuls to moisten if dry. Season with salt and pepper, sprinkle with chopped fennel fronds, and serve, passing additional cheese.

tortiglioni-melanzana.name: Tortiglioni Melanzana

ingredients.item: Salt and ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat; cook and stir onion and carrot until onion is translucent, about 5 minutes. Pour tomatoes into skillet and sprinkle sugar, salt, and black pepper into mixture. Bring to a simmer, turn heat to low, and simmer while you complete remaining steps. Stir occasionally.
Heat vegetable oil in a separate skillet over high heat and fry eggplant slices in the hot oil until lightly browned, about 2 minutes per side. Drain eggplant slices on paper towels.

Fill a large pot with lightly salted water and bring to a boil. Stir in tortiglioni pasta and return to a boil. Cook pasta uncovered, stirring occasionally, until cooked through, but still firm to the bite, about 11 minutes; drain.
Mash tomato sauce with a fork until chunky and transfer to a large bowl. Stir in eggplant slices, Kalamata olives, Grana Padano cheese, mayonnaise, and pasta; stir lightly to incorporate.

tortiglioni-pasta-with-minced-meat-telec.name: Tortiglioni Pasta With Minced Meat

ingredients.item: Parmesan cheese chips and basil stems to garnish

instructions: Peel and peel the onion and garlic very well; also peel celery; peel and thinly grate the carrot. Put these ingredients in a large pot, add some olive oil, take it to the fire and let it saute very well.
De seguida, junte as carnes picadas, tempere com sal e pimenta e deixe cozinhar, em lume brando, durante 10 minutos e mexendo de vez em quando.
Add the wine and boil to evaporate the alcohol. Add the tomato pulp and let it cook for another 10 minutes. In the end, correct the seasonings.
Cook the dough in salt-tempered water for about 9 to 11 minutes. Drain, add to the meat preparation and wrap well. Serve hot, trimming with parmesan cheese chips and basil stems.

tortilla-lezzet.name: Tortilla
instructions: 1. Take a rough and medium of corn flour.
2. Get a solid dough by pouring salt and warm water from the ground and stirring.
3. Remove parts in small balls from dough.
4. Prepare a 10 stagnation yufka with each wholesale oxlava.
5. Cook two sides on Teflon pan or sheet.

tortilla-bowl-southwestern-salad.name: Tortilla Bowl Southwestern Salad
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (180°C).
Pour the vegetable oil (1 teaspoon per bowl) into medium (1.2 quart) oven-proof bowls and rub around to coat the surface. Press each tortilla into a greased bowl.
Bake for about 10 minutes, until golden brown. Let the tortilla bowls cool.
Make 3 cuts lengthwise on each of the romaine hearts, remove the stems, and chop into smaller pieces. Rinse, drain, and place in a large salad bowl.
Dice the tomatoes and add them to the bowl with the romaine.
Dice the onion and add it to the salad.
Cut the avocados in half, remove the pits, and dice. Add to the salad.
Add the corn and black beans.
In a small bowl or liquid measuring cup, combine the olive oil, lime juice, garlic, cumin, red pepper flakes, cilantro, salt, and pepper. Mix well.
Pour the dressing over the salad and toss well.
Fill each tortilla bowl with the salad.
Enjoy!

tortilla-de-patatas.name: Tortilla De Patatas

ingredients.item: Kosher salt plus more for seasoning

instructions: Place potatoes in a large colander, sprinkle with 2 tsp. salt, and toss to coat. Let stand for 30 minutes. Pat dry with paper towels.
Heat oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add potatoes and cook, turning occasionally, until crisp-tender, 10–15 minutes.

Add onion to potatoes in skillet; cook until onion and potatoes are soft, 5–8 minutes. Using a slotted spoon, transfer mixture to a large bowl; let cool slightly. Strain oil into a heatproof bowl; set aside.
Add eggs to potato mixture; season with salt. Wipe out skillet. Heat ¼ cup reserved oil over medium-high heat (chill remaining oil for another use). Add egg mixture, arranging potatoes to submerge.

Reduce heat to medium; cook tortilla until almost set (it will be slightly runny on top), 25–30 minutes. Invert onto a large plate. Slide into skillet browned side up. Cook until golden on bottom and cooked through, about 4 minutes more. Serve warm or at room temperature. Cut into wedges.
DO AHEAD: Tortilla can be made 2 hours ahead. Let stand at room temperature.

tortilla-dessert-cups.name: Tortilla Dessert Cups
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375˚F (190˚C).

In a small bowl, combine the sugar and cinnamon. Butter each side of the tortillas, sprinkle with cinnamon sugar, and cut into even quarters, making 12 pieces. Place 2 pieces in 6 cups of a muffin tin and push down so that they create a cup shape. Bake for 13-15 minutes, or until crisp. Remove the cups from the oven and allow to cool in the tin.
In a large bowl, beat together heavy cream, vanilla, and sugar with an electric hand mixer until stiff peaks form.
Assemble the cups by placing a spoonful of whipped cream in each toasted cup. Top the whipped cream with fresh fruit of your choice.
Enjoy!

tortilla-espanola.name: Tortilla Española
instructions: Heat 1 Tbsp. oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add onion, season with salt, and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and dark brown, 35–40 minutes. Let cool slightly.

Meanwhile, heat potatoes and remaining 2 cups oil in a medium saucepan over medium-high heat until oil around potatoes begins to bubble; reduce heat to medium and cook until potatoes are tender but have not taken on any color, 10–12 minutes. Drain potatoes, reserving oil. Season potatoes with salt and let cool slightly.
Combine eggs, onion, potatoes, and ¼ cup reserved potato cooking oil in a large bowl and gently beat with a fork.

Heat 3 Tbsp. reserved potato cooking oil in a 10” nonstick skillet over medium heat (reserve remaining oil for another use). Add egg mixture and cook, lifting at edge and tilting skillet to let uncooked egg run underneath, until bottom and edge of tortilla are set but center is still wet.
Set a large plate on top of skillet. Swiftly invert tortilla onto plate, then slide back into skillet, cooked side up. Cook until center is just set, about 2 minutes longer. Cut into wedges.

tortilla-pizza.name: Tortilla Pizza

ingredients.item: Salt and ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Place tortilla on a baking sheet. Brush with olive oil; sprinkle garlic powder, salt, and pepper on top.
Bake in the preheated oven until golden, 3 to 5 minutes.
Remove from the oven and spread tomato sauce on top; arrange chicken breast, green bell pepper, and green onions over tomato sauce. Sprinkle with mozzarella cheese.
Bake in the preheated oven until cheese is melted, about 5 minutes. Sprinkle oregano over cheese. Slice pizza into wedges using a pizza cutter.
Enjoy.

tortilla-rolls-stuffed-with-tuna-pequere.name: Tortilla Rolls Stuffed With Tuna
tortilla-rolls-stuffed-with-tuna-pequere.original_name: Rollitos de tortilla rellenos con atún
instructions: We batified his balls with a pint of salt. We put some extra virgin olive oil into a frying pan, and we make the omelette, it should be quite widely spread, to be fine and great.

We let the omelette get cold and we put with the cream cheese, we put the green sprouts and at the centre the broken tuna. We roll and wrap with foil paper, squeezing and closing it as if it were a candy.
We put them in the refrigerator till we serve and we'll have to cut them up to get our most delicious omelette rolls filled with tuna.

tortilla-soup.name: Tortilla Soup

ingredients.item: Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper

ingredients.prep: or 2 cups frozen, thawed corn kernels
halved cherry tomatoes, avocado wedges, queso fresco or mild fetat

instructions: Bring chicken, onion, carrot, garlic, chipotles, jalapeño, and 16 cups water to a boil in a large pot; skim foam from surface. Reduce heat to medium and simmer, skimming the surface frequently, until chicken is cooked through, about 1 hour.

Transfer chicken to a plate. Strain broth into another large pot. Return chiles to broth, if a spicier broth is desired; discard remaining solids. Shred chicken meat; discard skin and bones. Transfer chicken meat to a medium bowl and set aside.

Meanwhile, set pot with strained broth over medium heat and add cilantro sprigs. Bring broth to a simmer; cook until reduced to 8 cups, about 1 hour. Discard sprigs and chiles, if using. Stir in 3 Tbsp. lime juice. Season with salt, pepper, and more lime juice, if desired. Add chicken to broth. DO AHEAD: Can be made 2 days ahead. Chill until cold, then cover and keep chilled.

Attach deep-fry thermometer to the side of a large cast-iron skillet or other heavy skillet. Pour oil into skillet to a depth of 1". Heat over medium heat until thermometer registers 350°–360°. Working in batches, fry tortilla strips, turning occasionally, until crisp and golden brown, 2–3 minutes per batch. Using a slotted spoon, transfer to paper towels to drain. Season with salt.

Cook corn over a gas flame or under a broiler, turning occasionally, until charred in spots. Let stand until cool enough to handle. Cut kernels from cobs in strips. Discard cobs. (Alternatively, cook kernels in a large skillet over high heat until charred in spots.)
Rewarm broth with chicken. Divide soup among bowls. Top generously with corn, tortilla strips, cilantro, tomatoes, avocado, and crumbled queso fresco.

tortilla-soup-mexican-recipe-comedera.name: Tortilla Soup, Mexican Recipe

ingredients.item: 1 taza de queso blanco fresco (para cortar)
Pimienta negra molida o pimienta blanca

instructions: We'll start with the chicken breast. Now with the clean meat, we'll warm up a pot with just more than a liter of water and we'll put it to cook with a touch of salt, a phis and a leaf of bay.
Mientras se cocina el pollo, podemos aprovechar para preparar otros ingredientes. En el caso de las tortillas, las cortaremos en cintas.
We will also cut the onion (peeling) into cubes and in the case of garlic teeth, we will peel them and mince them finely.
Then we'll heat up a frying pan with oil and high heat. With great care, we will fry the tortilla juiana, just as long as necessary to get them to golden and get crispy.
Then we'll get our omelets out of the oil. It's best to move them to a plate covered with absorbing paper to remove their excess fat.
Take care of the oil and hot frying pan to also fry the pieces of onion and garlic, this time with fire between and under.
Meanwhile, in a hot water bowl, remove the pasilla chilies, already clear. Instead, add them already cut into pieces and without seeds (except if you love the strong spicy).
At the same time, stay focused so that after about 15 or 20 minutes of boiling, you remove the chicken from the heat and its pot and only retain the stock.
Wash the tomato and cut them into small pieces. When the onion of the frying pan begins to become clear, it incorporates these pieces and also adds two of the moist chiles.
Mix well all the frying pan ingredients, cook five more minutes and then put them to a beaker or kitchen robot. Produce them to get a tomato purée as homogenous as possible.
Then we have to add the purée to the broth. Mix well and let it cook till it's broken. Then put the fire down to a minimum.

Take care of the frying pan where the tomatoes were cooked, to fry the third of the pasilla chilies, already cut into strips. It's only necessary to cook quickly to mark the taste and have a somewhat crisp texture.

Reserve the fried chili and prepare the other ingredients to decorate soup plates. We'll have to cut the avocado into cubes as well as the cheese. It measures a cup of the latter, already in pieces, to get as much as necessary.
Salpimenta las cintas de tortilla.

At the time of serving, we will place a portion of the decoration ingredients at the bottom of each plate: some fried omelette ribbons, some cheesebes and avocado, some fried chili strips. If you wish, a little chicken breast cut into pieces or cuts may be included. Then you have to put the soup onto each plate and at the end, decorate the surface with another little tortilla, avocado, cheese and chili, and additionally a touch of milk cream and some drops of lemon juice (optional).

tortillas-de-harina-flour-tortillas.name: Tortillas De Harina (flour Tortillas)
instructions: Mix the flour and pork lard with your fingers until the mixture is the texture of coarse sand. Then add the salt with the hot (but not scalding) water and knead well. If the dough is too sticky, add a little more flour; if it’s a little dry, add more hot water. The kneaded dough should be slightly tacky but not sticking to the bowl. Cover with plastic wrap and let sit at room temperature for 1 hour.

Divide the dough into 8 pieces and roll each one into a round ball. Roll each ball between sheets of parchment paper or plastic wrap into as thin a round as possible (the rounds will contract a bit when you stop rolling). As you roll each one, place it on a hot nonstick skillet or griddle over medium heat.

Cook for about 30 seconds, or until the tortilla starts to puff. Then flip and cook another 30 seconds. It should be cooked through and just speckled with golden brown spots. As the tortillas cook, place them in your serving dish covered with a dish towel, which will help keep the tortillas soft and pliable.

tortillas-in-the-oven-argiro.name: Tortillas In The Oven
instructions: Cut the vegetables into thin slices.
Heat the olive oil and butter in a small pan.
Sauté the onion for 3 ́, add the peppers, sauté for 2 ́, add the garlic broken and sauté for 1' extra.
Salt and pepper. Allow the vegetables to cool for 5 minutes.
We spread the pies on the work bench.
Butter the baking pan, add half the tomato to the baking pan.
Brush with melted butter on both sides the tortillas.
Fill with the vegetables, equalize the cheese and turkey tortillas.
Wrap and touch with the compound down on the baking pan.
After wrapping all the tortillas, pour with the rest of the chopped tomato, sprinkle with the graviera and finally pour with the cream.
We salt and pepper at will.
Bake in a well preheated oven in the air at 200 °C medium grill for 20 ́.
Garnish with chopped parsley.
Serve immediately before the tortillas cool while they are juicy and delicious, with green salad.

tortillas-with-vegetables-and-cream-chee.name: Tortillas With Vegetables And Cream Cheese
tortillas-with-vegetables-and-cream-chee.original_name: Τορτίγια με λαχανικά και τυρί κρέμα
instructions: For tortilla with vegetables and cream cheese, first cut all vegetables either in thin slices or sticks.
Spread the pies on the counter and rub them with the cream cheese.
We equalise the hearts of lettuce and vegetable sticks.
Wrap and serve.

tory-burchs-south-side.name: Tory Burch's South Side
instructions: Muddle mint leaves with simple syrup in a shaker. Add gin and lemon juice; fill shaker with ice. Shake until frosty. Strain into an ice-filled glass; top off with a splash of club soda and a mint sprig.

toscano-soup.name: Toscano Soup

ingredients.item: Idahoan® roasted garlic flavored mashed potatoes
Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Cook the bacon in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Drain on a paper towel.
Crumble and set aside.
Remove the turkey Italian sausage links from the casing and place in the Dutch oven along with the diced onion.
Saute until the onion is soft and the sausage is cooked.
Add the garlic, stirring frequently for 45 seconds.
Add the chicken stock, milk, and potatoes.
Add Swiss chard or kale along with the crushed red pepper, sea salt and fresh cracked black pepper to taste.
Cook on medium low for 15 minutes or until hot.
Ladle into soup bowls and garnish with crumbled bacon.

toshis-noodles-with-seared-sea-scallops.name: Toshi's Noodles With Seared Sea Scallops
instructions: Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook spaghetti in the boiling water, stirring occasionally, until tender yet firm to the bite, about 6 minutes. Drain and set aside.
Meanwhile, combine broccoli with 2 to 3 tablespoons water in a microwave-safe bowl; cover tightly. Microwave on high until tender, 3 to 4 minutes. Drain any liquid; set aside.
Combine soy sauce, mayonnaise, chili crisp, gochujang, sugar, garlic powder, and ginger in a bowl.

Heat oil and butter in a wok over medium heat until butter is sizzling but before it turns brown. Add onion; cook for 1 minute. Add scallops; cook and stir until seared, about 3 minutes. Add steamed broccoli; cook 1 to 2 minutes. Pour soy sauce mixture over top, add spaghetti, and toss until combined and heated through, 2 to 3 minutes.
Garnish with green onion and serve.

toss-together-broccoli-salad.name: Toss-together Broccoli Salad
instructions: Mix the broccoli, Cheddar cheese, onion, bacon, sunflower seeds, and raisins in a large bowl. Whisk mayonnaise, vinegar, and sugar together in a separate bowl; pour over the broccoli mixture and toss to coat.
Cover bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate at least 1 hour before serving.

tostadas-de-tinga.name: Tostadas De Tinga
instructions: Add water and chicken breasts to a crockpot with ½ of an onion, 2 garlic cloves, and a pinch of salt. Turn the crockpot on to a simmer.
While chicken is cooking, in a blender, blend ½ onion, tomatoes, 2 garlic cloves, salt, chipotles, and water. Once finished, in a skillet over medium heat, saute julienned onions until transparent.
Once cooked, add chicken and sauce and cook for 15 minutes to combine.
Lay a tostada shell on a flat surface, top with chicken mixture and desired toppings/garnishes. Repeat with remaining tostada shells.
Serve.

tostadas-with-chicken-tinga-and-black-be.name: Tostadas With Chicken Tinga And Black Beans
instructions: First, prep the spicy tomato sauce: Peel and finely chop 1 medium white onion; set aside ½ cup for serving. Transfer half of remaining chopped onion to a large saucepan (you’ll use the rest of the onion for the beans). Peel and finely chop 4 garlic cloves. Transfer to saucepan. Finely chop 1 canned chipotle chile in adobo sauce and add to pan along with 2 Tbsp. adobo sauce (use 1 Tbsp. if you’re sensitive to heat; you can store remaining chiles individually in the freezer to use later.)

Add 1 15-oz. can fire-roasted tomatoes and 2 Tbsp. chili powder and stir to combine. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat. Nestle 2 skin-on, bone-in chicken breasts into mixture, reduce heat to medium-low, and bring liquid to a simmer. Cover saucepan and cook until chicken is cooked through and shreds easily with two forks, 25–35 minutes.

Meanwhile, make the beans: Slice 4 oz. smoked bacon into thin strips about ¼" wide and transfer to a large skillet. Add remaining chopped onion and cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until fat has rendered, bacon is crispy, and onion is browned and tender, 8–10 minutes.

Increase to medium-high and add 2 15-oz. cans black beans and their liquid (that's right—don't drain them!). Bring to a boil and cook, stirring occasionally, until liquid is reduced slightly, about 5 minutes.

Using a potato masher (or a wooden spoon, if you're in a pinch), smash beans until almost no whole beans remain and mixture is thick, smooth, and creamy, like the consistency of sour cream. Remove from heat, taste and season with salt if necessary, and let cool slightly; the refried beans will thicken as they sit.

When chicken is finished cooking, use tongs to transfer to a plate and let cool slightly. While chicken cools, increase heat to medium-high and simmer sauce, uncovered and stirring occasionally, until it looks like a thick and chunky salsa, about 5 minutes. Let cool slightly in pan.

Remove skin from chicken breasts and separate meat from bones; discard skin and bones. Shred meat into small pieces and transfer to a medium bowl. Pour warm salsa over chicken, season with 1½ tsp. salt, and toss to coat.
Assemble your tostadas: Use your hands to crumble 4 oz. queso fresco into small pieces (about the size of dried lentils).
Spread about ⅓ cup refried beans over 1 tostada. Top with about ½ cup chicken tinga.
Repeat with as many tostadas as you think you’ll eat (the separate components will keep well in the fridge). Top with queso fresco, cilantro, and reserved chopped onion.

tostones-twice-fried-green-plantains-wit.name: Tostones (twice-fried Green Plantains) With Mayo Ketchup Dipping Sauce
instructions: Combine mayonnaise, ketchup, and garlic in a small bowl. Cover and refrigerate sauce for at least 30 minutes before serving.

Heat oil in a deep fryer or large saucepan to 177°C (175 degrees C). Fry plantains in hot oil until golden but not browned, 5 to 7 minutes. Drain on a paper towel-lined plate. Allow plantains to cool slightly.

Smash each plantain in a tostonero or with the bottom of a clean can. Fry flattened plantains again in hot oil until golden and crisp, 5 to 7 minutes more. Season with garlic salt and serve with sauce.

tostones-with-green-sauce.name: Tostones With Green Sauce

ingredients.prep: canola, or safflower; for frying, about 2 cups)

instructions: Wash and dry ½ bunch cilantro. Coarsely chop leaves and stems—you should have about 1 loosely packed cup. Trim roots and top inch of green parts of 4 scallions; discard. Cut scallions into 1" pieces. Cut ½ habanero chile in half lengthwise and remove seeds from one half (or leave them in, if you don’t mind extra heat).

Transfer cilantro, scallions, and chile to a blender. Squeeze in juice from 2 limes; add 2 Tbsp. water. Season with a big pinch of salt. Blend on medium speed until cilantro and scallions are finely chopped, about 15 seconds.

Add 1 cup sour cream or Greek yogurt and blend on high speed, scraping down sides as needed, until sauce is pale green and frothy, about 1 minute. Pour into a serving bowl and chill while you fry the plantains (or cover and chill sauce up to 1 day).

Pour vegetable oil into a large (10") cast-iron skillet, Dutch oven, or other large heavy pot to a depth of ½". Heat oil over medium-high until an instant-read thermometer registers 375°, 7–8 minutes.

Meanwhile, prep the plantains: Trim off both ends of the plantains. Using the tip of your knife, score skin of a plantain lengthwise down its ribs, rotating and repeating until you’ve scored the plantain all the way around. Make sure your cut extends down to the flesh of the plantain, but try not to cut into the flesh itself. Peel plantain along your score marks, then cut crosswise into ½"-thick coins.

Line a rimmed baking sheet with a wire rack, or with a double layer of earth-friendly paper towels. Working in 2 batches, fry plantains, turning occasionally to ensure they don’t stick to each other or to the pan, until golden and starting to crisp, about 2 minutes.
Using a fish spatula or slotted spoon, transfer plantains to prepared baking sheet. Repeat with remaining plantains.

Reduce heat to low to keep oil warm while you flatten the plantains. Using a meat mallet or a flat-bottomed dry measuring cup and working one by one, place plantain on cutting board and firmly press down on plantains to flatten them to less than ¼" thick; the edges should split and become craggy. (Do this while the plantains are still warm; otherwise, they become annoyingly difficult to flatten.)

Reheat oil to 375°. Working in 3 batches, fry flattened plantains, turning occasionally and letting oil return to 375° between batches, until dark golden brown and crisp, about 3 minutes. Transfer to prepared baking sheet; season immediately with salt (it sticks better when the tostones are hot!).

Transfer plantains to a platter. Serve with green sauce alongside for dipping.
Do ahead: Plantains can be fried 30 minutes ahead. Transfer to a wire rack set in a rimmed baking sheet and place in a 250° oven to keep warm.

tostones-with-white-bbq-sauce.name: Tostones With White Bbq Sauce

ingredients.prep: peeled and sliced into 2-in (5 cm) pieces

instructions: For the sauce: Whisk all ingredients together in a bowl, making sure to completely dissolve sugar.
For the tostones: Fill a cast iron skillet or dutch oven with about ¼" of oil and heat on medium high until the temperature reaches 163°C.
Fry the plantains in batches, cut side down, making sure not to crowd the pan, then flip, about 2 ½ minutes per side,
Remove plantains and place on a paper towel to drain. Turn your burner down to keep the oil hot but not burning. Try to maintain the 163°C.
Using the bottom of a glass, gently press down and flatten each plantain piece to ½" thick,
Transfer your mashed plantains back into the fry oil and fry again for 45 seconds per side.
Place your finished plantains on a paper towel to soak up excess oil and immediately sprinkle with kosher salt.
Serve.

totally-natural-maple-banana-bread.name: Totally Natural Maple-banana Bread
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Grease a loaf pan.
Combine flour, baking powder, salt, cinnamon, and pumpkin pie spice in a bowl.

Combine bananas and eggs in a second bowl and beat using an electric mixer until well combined. Mix applesauce, maple syrup, agave nectar, and vanilla extract into the bowl. Add banana mixture to flour mixture and mix using a spatula until fully moistened. Pour batter into the prepared loaf pan.

Bake in the preheated oven until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean, 80 to 90 minutes. Cool on a wire rack for 5 minutes. Run a table knife around the edges to loosen. Invert carefully onto a serving plate or cooling rack. Let cool completely.

toum-lebanese-garlic-spread.name: Toum (lebanese Garlic Spread)
instructions: Put garlic cloves in a food processor with the cutting blade in place. Pulse until smooth. Add salt. Use a spatula to push garlic into the middle of the bowl. Pulse again on low. Use the spatula to push down garlic again.

Using the drizzle adapter in the food processor, slowly pour in 1 1/2 tablespoons lemon juice on low speed. Pour in 1 cup oil; spread will emulsify and start to turn white. Turn off the processor and scrape down the sides. Add remaining lemon juice on low speed. Slowly add remaining 2 cups oil, processing until spread is thick and creamy.
Transfer garlic spread to a storage bowl with a lid and refrigerate until ready to use.

tourlou-briam-casserole-argiro.name: Tourlou Briam Casserole

ingredients.item: 2 onions, dried, cut into large frames
1 red pepper cut into large frames
1 green pepper cut into large frames
200 grifta cut into frame or thick trim
2 onions, dried, cut into large frames
1 red pepper cut into large frames
1 green pepper cut into large frames
200 grifta cut into frame or thick trim

instructions: Wash all the vegetables and cut them into frame.
Heat half the olive oil in a pot and blond the onion for 2 minutes.
Add the peppers, pumpkin, eggplant and sauté for 2-3 minutes.
Add the tomato cut into chops and potatoes.
Add salt, pepper and sugar.
Simmer for 20 minutes (without adding water).
Finally, pour the remaining olive oil.
Sprinkle with the herbs and the trim or the feta frames.
We withdraw from the fire.
Cover the pot for 5 minutes and serve with crispy fresh bread.
It's cold as a salad.

tourtiere-teleculinaria.name: Tourtiere

ingredients.item: 500 g de carne de porco picada
500 g de carne de vaca picada

instructions: Praise the garlic and onion, chopped, in a pot with the olive oil, then add the meats and saute them until they are loose. Add some potato flakes, stir again and remove from the fire. Season with salt, pepper, thyme, sage, cloves and cumin and stir.
Line a round tart (24 cm in diameter), with one of the rolls of broken dough, keeping the vegetable paper underneath. Pinch the bottom with a fork, fill with the previous preparation and smooth.

Cover with the other broken dough, joining the ends well, and make some cuts on the surface with a sharp knife. Brush with the yolk, previously dissolved in water, and take it to the oven, preheated at 200oC, for 20 minutes.
After this time, lower the temperature to 170oC and leave it in the oven for another 15 minutes or until golden. Remove, let it cool down a little, unshape and serve it hot or cold.

tourtiere-french-canadian-meat-pie.name: Tourtière (french Canadian Meat Pie)
instructions: Add flour, butter, and salt to the bowl of a food processor. Pulse until butter is pea-sized, about 30 seconds. Stir water and vinegar together in a small bowl; drizzle over flour mixture. Pulse until mixture is crumbly, but holds together when pinched, about 10 seconds. Drizzle in more cold water a little at a time if dough is too dry.
Transfer mixture to a work surface; form a ball, then flatten into a disc. Wrap in plastic; refrigerate for 1 hour.
Mix salt, black pepper, thyme, sage, cinnamon, ginger, nutmeg, allspice, mustard, clove, and cayenne together in a small bowl for the spice blend; set aside.

Add potato and 1 teaspoon salt to a medium saucepan; cover with cold water. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat. Reduce heat to low; simmer until tender, 10 to 15 minutes. Transfer potato to a large bowl with a slotted spoon; mash until smooth (reserve cooking water); set aside.

Melt butter in a large skillet over medium heat. Add onion and a pinch of salt. Cook until onions are golden, 10 to 15 minutes. Add celery, garlic, and spice blend to the skillet; stir until well combined, about 30 seconds.

Add pork, beef, and about 3/4 cup of reserved cooking water to the skillet for filling. Cook, stirring occasionally, until meat is brown, and most of the liquid has evaporated, about 45 minutes. Stir in potato, turn off the heat, and let cool.
Preheat the oven to 191°C (190 degrees C).

Divide dough into 2 pieces, 1 slightly larger than the other. Roll larger piece into a 12-inch circle on a lightly floured work surface. Place in a 9-inch, deep-dish pie plate. Roll remaining dough into an 11-inch circle; set aside.

Fill bottom crust evenly with meat mixture; whisk egg and water together in a small bowl for egg wash. Brush egg wash over edges of crust. Cover with top crust; press lightly around edges to seal. Trim excess dough; crimp edges. Cut small slits in top crust for steam to escape. Brush surface of pie with egg wash.
Bake in the preheated oven until crust is deep golden, about 1 hour. Let cool to almost room temperature before serving.

tourtiere-meat-pie-spices.name: Tourtière (meat Pie) Spices
instructions: Combine celery salt, pepper, savory, cloves, cinnamon, thyme, sage, and mustard powder in a small bowl. Mix well and store in a sealed jar.

tourtiere-du-shack.name: Tourtière Du Shack

ingredients.item: Whole black peppercorns plus freshly ground black pepper

instructions: Pulse flour, butter, and salt in a food processor until pea-size pieces of butter form. Transfer to a medium bowl. Add 1/4 cup ice water and stir just until shaggy clumps form, adding more water by teaspoonfuls if dry. Divide dough in half; flatten each half into a disk. Wrap disks in plastic wrap and chill for at least 2 hours. DO AHEAD: Dough can be made 2 days ahead. Keep chilled.

Preheat oven to 325°. Combine broth, 1/2 chopped onion, 1 chopped garlic clove, whole peppercorns, thyme, and bay leaves in a medium pot. Add pork shoulder; season with salt and pepper. Bring to a simmer over medium heat.
Cover pot. Transfer to oven; braise until pork shoulder is tender and shreds easily, about 2 hours. Remove from oven; let cool.

Transfer pork shoulder to a work surface. Shred meat with your fingers and transfer to a medium bowl. Strain pan juices through a fine-mesh sieve; add 1/2 cup juices to pork; discard solids in strainer.

Melt butter in a large skillet over medium heat. Add remaining 1 chopped onion and 3 chopped garlic cloves; cook, stirring often, until soft, 5–7 minutes. Add mushrooms; cook, stirring often, until almost all liquid is evaporated, 5–7 minutes. Add wine; stir, scraping up browned bits. Bring to a boil; cook, stirring often, until liquid is almost evaporated, about 5 minutes.

Add ground pork, cinnamon, and cloves. Cook, stirring to break up into small pieces, until pork is cooked through, about 5 minutes. Add potato. Cook until potato is soft, about 10 minutes. Remove from heat. Stir in shredded pork with juices. Season to taste with salt and pepper; let cool slightly. Chill until cold, about 1 hour. DO AHEAD: Can be made 1 day ahead. Cover and keep chilled.

Roll out 1 dough disk on a lightly floured surface into a 12" round. Transfer to pie dish, leaving overhang. Fill with cooled meat mixture. Roll out remaining dough disk into a 10" round. Place dough over meat filling. Fold overhang over top crust and crimp edges. Brush crust with egg yolk. Cut three 2" slits in top crust. Chill for 1 hour.
Preheat oven to 400°. Bake tourti're for 30 minutes. Reduce heat to 350°; bake until crust is golden brown and filling is bubbling, 40–50 minutes. Let cool for 20 minutes before serving.

toyga-soup-recipe-lezzet.name: Toyga Soup Recipe

ingredients.item: 5 cups of water (can also be used in water or chicken water)
Placed dry beans or barbunya can be used.

If desired, küçük grain beans can also be added.

The consistency can be achieved with starch (fruit or wheat).

Olive oil, moonçiçek oil or plain oil (ghee).
Vegan version için full olive oil can be preferred.
Fresh mint leaves can be added instead of dry min.
Different aroma için thyme or derelate.

2. Cook the bug in a separate pot.
3. Büyüsolidate wheat, lentil and nohudu in a pot.

tozzetti.name: Tozzetti

ingredients.item: All-purpose flour plus more for work surface

instructions: Preheat oven to 325°. Whisk baking powder, salt, and 1 3/4 cups flour in a medium bowl; set aside. Using an electric mixer on medium-high, beat sugar and butter in a medium bowl until creamy, about 2 minutes. Add eggs one at a time, beating to blend between additions and occasionally scraping sides of bowl, until combined. Reduce speed to low, add dry ingredients, and mix just to blend. Fold in almonds.

Transfer dough to a lightly floured work surface and knead just to bring together, about 2 times. Form into a log about 14" long, 2" wide, and 1 inch thick. Place log on a parchment-lined baking sheet.
Bake until puffed, cracked in spots, and starting to brown, 25–30 minutes. Transfer baking sheet to a wire rack and let log cool.

Using a serrated knife, slice log diagonally 1/2-inch thick. Arrange slices on same sheet; bake until golden brown, 15–20 minutes. Turn and bake until other side is golden brown, 5–10 minutes longer. Transfer baking sheet to a wire rack. Let tozzetti cool.
DO AHEAD: Tozzetti can be made 1 week ahead. Store airtight at room temperature.

trabzon-oily-pita-lezzet.name: Trabzon Oily Pita

ingredients.item: 150 grams of cheese cheese grater or white cheese

instructions: 1. For dough; mix in a container by adding yeast, flour and water.
2. When it starts to get doughy consistency, add salt and knead dough until it achieves smooth consistency.
3. Relax over 30 minutes to swim with a clean cloth.
4. Separate the yeast dough into two equal parts and open it with your hands on the unified countertop.
5. Curl the edges of the dough in order not to flow out of the inner mortar.
6. Place cooking paper into the oven tray to be cool and open dough together space.
7. For inner mortar; choose two different cheeses and mix them in one container.
8. Add half glass of water on the cheese and mix again.
9. The inner mortar you prepare, add the dough to the middle, and evenly broadcast.
10. Add small butter pieces on cheese mixture.
11. 11. Take the egg yolks with the help of brush on the edges of the pidenin.
12. Pre-heated 220-grade oven, bake the pides up to 10 minutes.

14 July Trabzon oily pidesini can serve in a warm way.

18. You can ensure that the yolks of the egg are more crispy than the edges of the dough.
19. September If you wish, you can sprinkle with the dough edges.

trabzon-style-kuymak-recipe-lezzet.name: Trabzon Style Kuymak Recipe

ingredients.item: 150 gr trabzon cheese or wire cheese (rejected)
Instead of butter, you can use half-half olive oil and butter mixture; it gives both lighter and different flavor.
Vegan bir seçenek için bitkisel margarin tercih edebilirsiniz.
Traditional kuymak için corn flour is used, but you can also try full wheat flour or pollen.
A more creamy consistency can mix için corn flour and some irmic.
You can completely change the water with süt, get more creamy and rich taste.
Süt can be used instead of semi-half water and süt mixture.
Trabzon cheese can be used in wire cheese, cheese or mozzarella instead of cheese.
Daha hafif bir versiyon için az tuzlu peynirler tercih edilebilir.
By increasing the amount of cheese, you can get a more dense and stranded quantum.
Kuymak is often served simple, but you can add göre small amount of pepper or red pepper to your taste.
With fresh herbs (maydanoz, dereotu) you can add a different gö rationality and aroma.

instructions: 1. melt butter in a medium-sized pan, pay attention to heating more.
2. Add the flour of corn to the i&ccedil of the butter, and roast 2–3 minutes in the middle fire; smellç stir up to the fire.
3. Water or sütü gradually add and do not tolerate için sümixed.
4. After boiling the mixture, add grated Trabzon cheese or wire cheese.
5. Mix the cheese completely until the melt and the mixture begins to leave the wire.
6. The wire wire wire wire should be separated, consistency and hot served.
7. You can serve with fresh bread on the side.
8.  Afiyet olsun.

trader-vic-mai-tai-cocktail.name: Trader Vic Mai Tai Cocktail

ingredients.item: Fluid ounces 17-year old j. wray nephew jamaican rum

instructions: Fill a rocks glass with 1 cup crushed ice. Freeze the glass.
Cut a strip of zest from lime with a peeler; set aside. Halve lime; juice both halves into a cocktail shaker.
Add rum, Orgeat, Curaçao, and rock candy syrup to the cocktail shaker; add 1 cup ice cubes. Cover and shake until the outside of the shaker has frosted.
Place reserved lime zest into the chilled rocks glass; strain in mai tai mixture. Float mint sprig on edge of the glass.

traditional-almond-tart-teleculinaria.name: Traditional Almond Tart
instructions: Ligue o forno a 180 graus. Unte uma tarteira com margarina e reserve.
Prepare the dough: in a bowl, beat the butter with sugar. Then add the eggs and flour, slowly and always beating very well. Finally, add the milk, always knocking.
Disponha a massa anterior na tarteira e alise. Leve ao forno durante 15 minutos, retire e reserve.
Prepare the filling: put sugar, butter, milk and lemon juice in a pot and stir until it is well dissolved. Add the almond, take it to the fire and let it thicken, always stirring.
Lay the previous mixture on the tarteira, on the already cooked base, and bring it back to the oven until it is golden. Remove from the oven, let it cool and serve.

traditional-andalusian-gazpacho-pequerec.name: Traditional Andalusian Gazpacho

ingredients.item: 3 cucharadas de aceite de oliva virgen extra

instructions: Lavamos muy bien los tomates y el pimiento ya que los vamos a triturar con piel.
We put in the beaker's glass (or if you have an American cook robot or glass), the crisped pepper, the chopped tomato, the pitted and crumped cucumber, the pitted bread and onions and in quarters.
We have added salt and vinegar and we have been tripping everything for several minutes till we get a very fine cream.
We added some extra virgin olive oil and we mixed again.

At this moment you can serve them as well or if you want more fluids, add water and mix them up again. Just put him in the refrigerator and let him get cold. Mix again at serving and accompanying with cuts of vegetables cut into small cubes, so that every guest will serve his or her best.

traditional-appalachian-spoon-bread.name: Traditional Appalachian Spoon Bread
instructions: Preheat oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Grease an oven-proof cast iron Dutch oven or a deep baking dish.

Heat milk and cornmeal in the top of a double boiler over simmering water, stirring frequently, until thick and creamy, about 5 minutes. Remove cornmeal mixture from heat and stir in butter and 1 teaspoon salt until well combined.
Beat egg yolks, sugar, and 1/2 teaspoon cream of tartar together in a bowl until smooth; stir into cornmeal mixture.

Beat egg whites, 1/8 teaspoon cream of tartar, and 1 pinch salt together in a separate bowl using an electric mixer until soft peaks form. Lift your beater or whisk straight up: the egg whites will form soft mounds rather than a sharp peak. Gently fold egg white mixture into cornmeal mixture until well mixed and fluffy; pour into prepared Dutch oven.
Bake in the preheated oven until golden brown with a moist center, about 30 minutes. Allow to rest for 10 to 15 minutes before serving.

traditional-arantist-lentils-argiro.name: Traditional Arantist Lentils

ingredients.item: 1/2 mash fresh mint or 1 tablespoon dried mint
1/2 mash fresh mint or 1 tablespoon dried mint

instructions: First boil the lentils with plenty of water for 5 minutes.
Drain in a colander and discard the first dark water.
In a deep pot, add the olive oil and sauté the onion for 3 minutes.
Add the garlic in slices and sauté for another minute.
Add the carrot, paste (optional), laurel and 1.5 liters of water.
Boil the lentils over low to medium heat until they soften for about 40 minutes.
Add the ground pasta to the pot and boil for 5 to 7′ until the pasta softens.
We're checking the salt and pepper.
After the aradist lentils are boiled with pasta, add the caramelized onions, sprinkle with the mint and mix the flavors.
Serve the aradistic lentils warm.

traditional-asturian-pot-step-by-step-pe.name: Traditional Asturian Pot Step By Step
traditional-asturian-pot-step-by-step-pe.original_name: Pote Asturiano tradicional paso a paso

ingredients.item: 400 g de fabas asturianas (2 vasos aprox.)
200 g de tocino o panceta curada
300 g de costillas de cerdo carnosas

instructions: La noche anterior, ponemos los 400 g de fabes a remojo en abundante agua durante al menos 12 horas para que se hidraten bien.

The next day we scrubbed and put them in a large pan covered with cold water with high heat. When we start to boil, we add some cold water to "scare them" (this helps the leprechability to avoid releasing skin) and we defrosted the surface with a frying pan so that the stock will be clear.

When she's breaking up and boiling for a second time, we put out the chorizo, the cheesebirk, the 200 g of pancette and 300 g of ribs. We come down with low-light fire and let them cook about 60 minutes. Remember to avoid inserting the spoon to remove. It's best to move the casserole with soft pods so as to avoid breaking the pieces.
Mientras se cocinan las legumbres, lavamos el manojo de berzas, quitamos los tallos y cortamos las hojas en juliana fina.
On the other hand, we pour the 3 big potatoes and cut them up (breaking the end of the cut) to let go and get fat.
We have been using clear and drained verza leaves, and at first you can think of a lot but they soon reduce their size. We cook with soft heat about 30 minutes.
Incorporamos las patatas junto un poco de sal y dejamos cocinar otros 20 minutos.

Finally, in a frying pan we put 3 pitted and minced garlic teeth with some extra virgin olive oil, frying and as soon as they start to golden we put the frying pan aside from the fire, we put a tablespoon of peppermint, we mix and add to the pan. We continue to cook another 10 minutes.
Rectificamos de sal al final.  Dejar reposar unas horas. Antes de servir, sacamos las carnes, las troceamos y las devolvemos a la olla.

traditional-baked-egg-flan-recipe-comede.name: Traditional Baked Egg Flan Recipe
traditional-baked-egg-flan-recipe-comede.original_name: Receta tradicional de flan de huevo al horno
instructions: It starts with the production of caramel. In a pot with medium heat serve sugar and water. Don't move or mix. Wait till you start changing your color, as soon as you look golden, remove from the fire and pour into the mold for the flan. Reserve.
In a bowl, pour your eggs and sugar. Bat till the ingredients are incorporated. Add milk and bat again.
Fold the flan mixture into the caramelized mold. Cover with foil paper, making sure that the banks are well covered.
Turn on the oven at 180 ° C. He looks for a baker that's bigger than his flan mold. Put the flaner in the bin and pour water with 2-3 fingers of water. Hornea for 50 minutes or up to an hour.
When the flan is at its point, remove from the oven and let completely cool. Once it's cold, come into the refrigerator and let it stand for 4 hours.
Una vez que pase el tiempo de enfriamiento, retira el papel aluminio, pasa un cuchillo por las orillas, coloca un plato grande encima, voltea y desmolda. Disfruta.

traditional-banda-rice-recipe-comedera.name: Traditional Banda Rice Recipe
traditional-banda-rice-recipe-comedera.original_name: Receta del arroz a banda tradicional

ingredients.item: 400 gr de arroz (puede ser redondo o bomba)

instructions: To start with, we'll leave ready the seafood we're using. The sepia and'a should be clear and clear, so that we can put them into notebooks. In the case of shrimp, if for some reason they aren't already pitted, this is the time to put ourselves to that.
Then we'll care about the vegetables. We'll wash the tomatoes and then we'll rhyme them. Whether or not to preserve skin depends on every cook.
En el caso de los dientes de ajo, los pelaremos y los picaremos menudamente. Si cuentas con un triturador en casa, puedes triturarlos en lugar de pelarlos.
In the case of onion, we will peel and mince it into very small cubes.
Luego, agregaremos un chorro generoso de aceite en una paella y lo calentaremos a fuego medio-bajo.
When the oil's hot, we'll add the minced squid. We'll let it get golden about 5 minutes, often stirring and keeping them from burning.
After this time, we'll have an onion and garlic. We'll cook another 5 minutes, and we'll have a few more minutes.

The following will be to add the grated tomato. We'll mix with the ingredients that are already in the paella. We'll spread it across the surface so that it's cooked evenly. We'll let them cook them till they lose most of them.
Then we'll have salt and pepper to taste as well as peppermint. We'll get the rice and mix them up and let them cook for a few more minutes.

1 After two minutes we will have a fish stock. We will soon add a touch of saffron or dyes to give our plate some color. We will also mix everything and test the broth to check the date. If necessary, we'll fix with a little more salt.

1 Now we shall carry the fire half and let the rice be cooked for 10 minutes. The goal is for the liquid to evaporate and the flavors to be focused (but care should be taken that the rice does not completely dry).

1 After this time, we will put up with the shrimp and put the fire to a minimum and let the rice finish cooking. Now we have to wait till the whole broth and the grain of rice are dry at its point, which will take about 15 minutes, or a little more (depending on the power of the kitchen, the thickness of the paella base, among other factors).
1 When cooking time is up, put out the fire and let the rice rest for a few minutes before serving.

traditional-basque-bonito-marmitako-reci.name: Traditional Basque Bonito Marmitako: Recipe
traditional-basque-bonito-marmitako-reci.original_name: Marmitako de bonito vasco tradicional: receta fácil
instructions: To start preparing the marmitako, the most important thing is to take care of the beautiful. It should be clear and clear with no skin and thorns and ready for us to cut with a good sharp knife.
Then we're gonna need a big, nonstick frying pan. That's where we'll get an oil jet.
Once the oil's hot, let's add the pieces of pretty. We'll cook them only what's necessary to seal them throughout their surface. That process should be very quick.
Then we will remove the fish from the frying pan and optionally we can place them on a plate covered with an absorbent paper sheet to remove excess fat.

Then we'll get care of the vegetables. Before we start with this stage, we'll put up a small pot with half full of water. We'll do this and cook the tomatoes quickly later. Then we'll peel the onion and get them into small pieces. In the case of potatoes, we will also skin them and mince them into large cubes.

Enseguida nos haremos cargo de los pimientos. Los lavaremos bien, retiraremos la cabeza y los abriremos para sacar las semillas y cortar la vena, o parte interna blanca. Luego, los picaremos en cuadros.
Then we'll wash the tomatoes and with hot water in the pot, we'll let them cook for two minutes.
Pasado este tiempo, apagaremos la olla, sacaremos los tomates y con cuidado de no quemarnos, separaremos la piel del resto del fruto. Luego, los picaremos en trozos medianos.
The following will be to get a big saucer to cook the marmitako. We'll warm her up with a generous jet of oil at medium heat.

Once the oil's hot, we'll drink the onion for a few minutes, stirring constantly. After this time, we will add the pepper and let the whole be cooked 4 more minutes, stirring often and keeping them from burning.

After this time, we're going to get tomato and mix and cook for another couple of minutes. Then it will be time to add the pits of potatoes. We'll let them cook another 5 minutes, and then have salt and pepper with them to taste, before they mix them all back.

Now let's have a bath with the wine and cook 3 minutes so that the alcohol evaporate a little. After this time we'll have to put up with the fish stock. Ideally, cover the level of solids in the saucer. If this doesn't happen, we can add some water to make up for, or some more fumet if we have more available.
Then we'll let the set cook for 30 minutes. Special care to avoid evaporate to the point where the plate will dry.
Once this time's up, we'll check that the potatoes are tender. If for some reason this isn't the case, we'll let them cook some more minutes.
Later, we will check and if necessary we will correct with more salt or pepper. If we do this last, we'll mix everything up again.
Finally, we will add the pieces of fish to the pot, and we will let them cook another 5 minutes, just so that the pretty one gets hot.
That's all. Our beautiful marmitako's ready to serve.

traditional-bean-and-barley-soup-telecul.name: Traditional Bean And Barley Soup
traditional-bean-and-barley-soup-telecul.original_name: Sopa tradicional de feijão e cevada
instructions: Wash the barley and tear it down overnight. The next day, drain it, pass it by cold water and cook it in salt-tempered water for about 30 minutes. Drain and reserve.
Peel and roughly peel the onion and garlic; peel and cut the carrot into slices; peel and cut the turnip into cubes; wash the cabbage and cut it into strips; cut the sausage into slices.

Light a pot with a string of olive oil and saute onion and garlic in it. Add the carrot, turnip, kale and chorizo, season with salt and pepper, cover with water and let cook, in mild heat, until the vegetables are almost soft.
Then add the baked beans and barley and let it cook for another 5 minutes. Serve immediately.

traditional-biscuit-cake-teleculinaria.name: Traditional Biscuit Cake
instructions: In a bowl, mix the butter with powdered sugar, yolks and coffee. Hit everything very well until it gets creamy.
In a way to open, have alternate layers of cookies, previously passed by sugary coffee, and cream. Decorate the surface with the remaining cream and lead to cold.
At the time of serving, carefully unpack the cake and decorate with coffee beans.

traditional-bread-of-the-dead-comedera.name: Traditional Bread Of The Dead
instructions: We put in a bowl 2 cups of multi-use flour and put a hole in his middle to add 3 eggs, 3 egg yolks, 1 and a half tablespoons of fresh yeast, half tablespoons of lemon and 1 tablespoon of anises.
Movemos todo ligeramente y agregamos 1 taza de leche y 3/4 de taza de mantequilla. Amasamos durante unos 30 min hasta que la masa se despegue del recipiente.
Tapamos con film y dejamos fermentar 30 min.
Una vez fermentado cortamos la masa en 5 trozos.
Of the five pieces, 4 of them are made as a ball and with the other we will make 8 "bones" and 4 balls.
Ponemos las 4 bolas en una bandeja de horno y con agua ayudamos a pegar los huesos a la masa. Horneamos 30 min a 350 grados.
Newly baked we paint with butter and add sugar.
Enjoy!

traditional-cabbage-rolls-argiro.name: Traditional Cabbage Rolls

ingredients.item: 500 grits mixed (ari and pork or beef and pork)
500 grits mixed (ari and pork or beef and pork)

instructions: Clean the outer leaves from the cabbage and remove the stem with a knife.
Simmer the cabbage, with the hole that left the stem down, in a boiling pot of water. We make sure the water half covers the cabbage.
Boil for 7-10 minutes to soften slightly, and leave on a platter to cool.

traditional-camel-baba-teleculinaria.name: Traditional Camel Baba
instructions: Put the condensed milk in a container and heat it right in the microwave. Add the yolks one by one, stirring well between each addition.
On the other hand, beat the clears into a firm castle and wrap them in the previous preparation.
Lay the camel's drool on a large bowl, sprinkle it with roasted rolled almonds and bring it to the fridge until it serves.

traditional-catalan-cream-recipe-comeder.name: Traditional Catalan Cream Recipe
instructions: To start with, we'll separate the yolks from the clear from the eggs. Since for this recipe we will only need the yolks, we can keep the clear ones in a bowl in the refrigerator to prepare another recipe (and consume them quickly).

We'll also leave another ingredient ready to use: lemon. With a manual pellet, remove the skin into tapes, or as complete as you can. The rest of the lemon will be intact for another production (but while keeping it in the refrigerator).
Then, in a bowl, we will mix egg yolks with sugar and get a well integrated dough.
On the other hand, in a saucepan, we will warm up the milk and add the lemon shell with the cinnamon branch. At the time of pouring milk, we'll have to put some place in a cup.
Dejaremos que la leche se caliente hasta hervir, con el fuego a temperatura media, para asegurarnos de que no se queme o se pegue a la superficie de la olla.

At the same time, we will have to use them to dilute the maize starch into the other milk we had been saving at the plate. We'll mix up this way to make sure that the maicena doesn't make clumps in the liquid. Once this step has been achieved, we will add it to the rest of the milk.
After the milk has been boiled, we will remove it from the fire and remove the skin from the lemon and cinnamon branch.
We will immediately mix with the dough we had prepared earlier with egg and sugar.

After all ingredients are well integrated, get them back into the fire and cook them at low temperature, till they get creamy consistency. To achieve this, you will have to constantly remove them with a wooden scatula, and that will make the mixture become thick, little by little.

When you get the desired creamy consistency, remove the mixture from the fire. Retract them into individual containers you wish to use to serve. If you have traditional mud pits, so much better. Another option is to use a single large mold.
Let your dessert cool at room temperature, before passing them to the refrigerator. Then put him cold for a few hours and put him under care.

When you get to serve the Catalan cream, cover every bowl with a good sugar cape and quickly pass the fire of a blow, so that the sugar will burn or caramelish. That will give you the characteristic golden color of the surface of this dessert.

traditional-charro-beans-recipe-comedera.name: Traditional Charro Beans Recipe

ingredients.item: 250 g hot dogs or smoked chops (or ham)
5  jitomates o tomates rojos grandes y maduros

instructions: If the beans are no longer clear, we'll start by washing them and making sure that no residue remains.
Then we will get a big pan to cook the beans with abundant water (8 cups) and a touch of salt. For cooking, we'll count two hours.
Meanwhile, we will use them to cut half onion into cubes, sausage / choulette or ham into wheels or cubes and bacon into pieces.
We will also wash the tree chili, remove his branch and his seed and his vein and cut them into very small pieces.
En el caso de los dientes de ajo, los pelaremos y los picaremos muy finamente.

The last thing we'll get to get minced with are the chorizo, the tomato and the cilantro. In both cases, they should be very well washed (I prefer to remove the cilantro for some minutes in vinegar water).
Picaremos finamente las hojas de cilantro y respecto a los jitomates, los picaremos en trozos. Para el chorizo, lo picaremos en ruedas.

Then we'll warm up a frying pan to fry the bacon and onion and garlic pieces. We can take advantage of the natural fat that will release the bacon with the heat. If you need more fat, you can add some vegetable oil (I'd rather not).
After 4 minutes, we will put chili and pieces of sausage / choulette or ham into our cooking. We'll let everything cook 10 minutes, at medium low heat. Then we'll put out the mix and put it back.
After the two hours of cooking of the beans, we will check that they are soft (otherwise, we have to let them cook a little more).

Then, without turning off the cooking, we will add to the beans the mixture we have prepared earlier. We will also add cilantro, tomato, chorizo, half a teaspoon of powder, half a teaspoon of cumin powder, focused chicken stock and an additional touch of salt.
We'll mix well and let the beans cook for another 15 minutes, with low heat.
After this time, we will check the date of the beans. If necessary, we will fix with more salt, pepper or even chicken stock.
That's all. Our jars are ready to serve.

traditional-chicken-cobb-salad.name: Traditional Chicken Cobb Salad
instructions: Dressing: Whisk together French’s Dijon Mustard, shallots, lemon juice, sugar, salt, and pepper. Gradually whisk in the olive oil until dressing is fully emulsified. Set aside.
Rub chicken breasts with olive oil. Sprinkle generously with salt and pepper.

Heat up an oiled skillet over medium-high heat. Cook chicken until seared on the first side, 3–4 minutes, then flip the chicken. Cover with a lid to allow the chicken to cook through, 7–10 minutes. Once the chicken is cooked through, remove from pan and let rest for 5–10 minutes, then cut into slices.
Cut the hard-boiled eggs into quarters. Cut the bacon into 1-inch (2 ½ cm) pieces.
Arrange the lettuce on a large platter. On top of the lettuce, place small piles of the chicken, eggs, bacon, tomatoes, radishes, cucumbers, and avocado.
Enjoy!

traditional-chicken-milanese-in-the-air-.name: Traditional Chicken Milanese, In The Air Fryer, Or Baked
traditional-chicken-milanese-in-the-air-.original_name: Milanesa de pollo tradicional, en freidora de aire, o al horno

ingredients.item: Optional: a lemon chorrit and some grated cheese for bouncing

instructions: He cuts the 2 chicken breasts into steaks about half an inch. If something bad remains, you can hit with a deck or with the base of a glass and put them up. That's how they'll cook evenly and be more tender.
In a big bowl, beat up 3 eggs with some salt, pepper and 2 very fine minced garlic teeth. Also add a good handful of chopped fresh parsley and, if you like, a lemon chorrit.
Put the chicken steaks inside and move well so they get completely covered. Let them rest at least 15 or 20 minutes, but if you have time, an hour in the refrigerator greatly improves the result.

Meanwhile, prepare the padded bread (about 200 grams). If you mix it with some grated cheese, even better, because it's more tasty. Pass every stick from the adobo to the frosted bread and press with his hands to stick well.
If you want them to get more crispy, you can repeat the passage: egg again and bread pitted again.

Warm abundant soft olive oil in a large frying pan. When the oil's hot (about 170-3) and if you have thermometer, but if you don't, just bubble with a few pieces of bread), fry the milanese. About two or three minutes per side are usually sufficient, until they are golden and crispy.
Take them and place them on paper and cook them so that they run out their excess oil.

traditional-chicken-pie-argiro.name: Traditional Chicken Pie
instructions: Boil the chicken for 1 hour until it softens.
In the last quarter of the boil, pour the onions and leek into the broth.
Remove the chicken from the broth and allow it to cool, so that we can unscrew it.

traditional-chicken-shawarma-comedera.name: Traditional Chicken Shawarma

ingredients.item: 1 kg de pechuga de pollo en tiras
1 cda of ground pimenton or paprika
200 gr de lechuga  ((preferencia repollada o romana))
Tahini sauce ((you can buy them from Arab stores))

instructions: In bowl, we put the chicken strips and then we will add salt, pepper, turmeric, cumin, dusted garlic, ground or paprika. With the help of a tablespoon, we remove very well until the chicken's completely tipsy.
Dejaremos reposar nuestras tiras de pollo condimentadas por 1 hora para que todos los sabores se complementen.

Take onion and tomatoes and cut them into pieces or brunoa. Do the same with lettuce but in strips. Remember to use a plastic knife with a pair that doesn't get rusty If you don't have them, use your hands.

Finished with the tiling, you'll heat up a frying pan with oil, put garlic into sheets, sodas and later add chicken strips. Cooking, for about 20 minutes, the chicken should be salted, crisp and juicy inside.
Let the chicken stand till it's warm, we can't add it very hot to the bread, as it will break.
Take the Arab loaves or pat and let's have some. Pass them through the plate from place to place until they have some texture.
Later, you will add a portion of chicken to each of them, and then as many vegetables as you will and suit them with the taheni (or jonjoli) sauce.
Wrap up and have a delicious chicken shawma, ready for you to taste.
We're done!

traditional-chickpea-falafel-recipe-come.name: Traditional Chickpea Falafel, Recipe
traditional-chickpea-falafel-recipe-come.original_name: Falafel tradicional de garbanzos, receta fácil

ingredients.item: 1/4  de kilo de garbanzos remojados durante toda una noche

instructions: We'll start with a good run from the chickpeas that are already soaked. Then we'll wash them with plenty of cold water. I usually put them in a big paddle and soak them under the cold water jet. Keep in mind that the drier the chickpeas are after they have been drained, the better will be the consistency of the dough for falafels.
To continue, we will peel and pint the onion into 4 or 5 pieces. We'll also care about parsley and cilantro. We will wash his leaves well and finally we will peel his garlic tooth.

We'll put all these vegetables into a kitchen robot, including chickpeas, as well as a cup of water and jonjolí grains. Then we'll have a tiespoon (comine coffee powder and the same measure of ground pepper and salt).

We'll process all these ingredients till we get some kind of homogenous dough. Once ready, we should let her stand up and have more consistency. Ideally, leave her for a few hours, but if you're in a bad hurry, cut this time to 30 minutes.
After the rest time of the dough, form with small hands little balls a little flattened, till using all the preparations.

Warm a frying pan with abundant oil and fry every falafel, both sides. When each is golden and cooked, get him out of the frying pan with a scatula and let him stand on a plate covered with an absorbing paper sheet to absorb excess fat.
That's all. The Falafels are ready to eat.

traditional-childrens-recipe-teleculinar.name: Traditional Children's Recipe
instructions: Light a pot with water and salt, add the pumpkin and let it cook. Once cooked, drain it well, crush it and let it love. Add the milk, lemon shaving, yeast, eggs and flour and knead very well. Tape with a damp cloth and let it leave till it is double the volume.
Then remove pieces of dough and stretch it with your fingertips until it is very thin.
