Bring a frying pan with oil to the fire when it's hot enough to fry your children. Then remove and let it run down on absorbent paper. Pass them through yellow sugar and serve decorated to taste.

traditional-chilean-chacarero-sandwich-c.name: Traditional Chilean Chacarero Sandwich
traditional-chilean-chacarero-sandwich-c.original_name: Sándwich chacarero chileno al estilo tradicional

ingredients.item: 250 g of beef and veal in fine sheets (smooth posta, smooth loin or seating)
2  panes frica o similares (de miga blanda)

instructions: Lava los porotos verdes, retira los extremos y corta en tiras finas a lo largo.
It takes a pot with water and salt at boiling, and cooks the green 4-5 minutes till they are tender but still crispy. Drain and cool under a cold water jet to keep the color. Reserve.
Wash the tomato and cut it into thick slices so that it doesn't lose juice in the assembly.
Warm a frying pan or plate with medium and high heat with oil. He cooks his meat about 3 minutes and pounding while it's doring.
Open the loaves and if you want more texture, slightly twist the inside onto the frying pan.
Rig up the sandwich by first placing hot meat, followed by tomato slices and, above all, green porotes evenly distributed.
Cierra con la tapa del pan y sirve inmediatamente, antes de que la carne pierda temperatura.

traditional-chili-with-ground-turkey.name: Traditional Chili With Ground Turkey
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat; stir in ground turkey. Cook and stir until turkey is crumbly, evenly browned, and no longer pink. Drain and discard any excess grease.

Stir in onion, bell peppers, and jalapeños; cook and stir until onion softened and translucent, about 8 minutes. Add garlic, cumin, chili powder, sugar, salt, black pepper, and thyme. Continue to cook until fragrant, about 8 more minutes, stirring occasionally.

Stir in beef broth, pinto beans, red beans, diced tomatoes, tomato sauce, and tomato paste. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low. Simmer until thickened, about 45 minutes, stirring occasionally. For a thinner chili, cover the pot when simmering.

traditional-chocolate-mousse-teleculinar.name: Traditional Chocolate Mousse
instructions: Break the chocolate in pieces and melt it, along with the butter, in a sea bath.
Beat the yolks with the sugar, until it becomes off-white, and then add the chocolate to it, in yarn and always beating.
Finally, gently wrap the clears, previously beaten in castle. Lay the mousse on a bowl, decorate it with chocolate chips and lead to cold until it is firm.

traditional-christmas-fritters-teleculin.name: Traditional Christmas Fritters
instructions: Wipe the flour on the counter, add the sugar, butter, egg, yolks, lemon and brandy. Knead well until you get a homogeneous elastic consistency.
Extend the dough on the bench sprinkled with flour and cut it into Christmas shapes using own cutters.
Fry in abundant and hot oil, remove and drain on absorbent paper. Before serving, sprinkle the fried with powdered sugar.

traditional-cod-brandade-comedera.name: Traditional Cod Brandade

ingredients.item: 1/5  de litro de aceite de oliva suave

instructions: To start with, we'll make sure that the cod's drained and dry. We'll remove the skin and if so, thorns or other parts that interferes with the soft texture of his flesh, as well as beards.
If you decide to use the parsley, we have to wash them very well and then get them quite finely. We'll keep them in a small cup for decoration at the end of this recipe.
To continue, we will peel the garlic and mince it very finely, whether it's sheets or notebooks. It's better if you can shredder them.

Then we'll warm up a good frying pan or case with an oil jet, to fry the garlic we come from peeling and cutting. The fire shouldn't be high, because the idea is that the garlic will get slightly cold. We don't want her to get attached or burned.

When we see that garlic starts to cook, we'll have cod incorporated into cooking. We'll let them cook only what's necessary to soften (about 3 minutes, normally). Then we'll get everything out of the fire.
We'll get the cod out of the frying and crumbling with the help of a fork. To achieve smaller crumbs, we can use a mortar.
In the case of oil and garlic, we'll separate them. We will use a drainer to remove the pieces and save them for later. The oil will be clear for us to re-use at another step of the recipe.

Now we're going to put the cod meat back into a bowl and we're going to put the oil from the old frying, still hot. The goal is that fish sucks or absorbs fat and we start to emulsify our pits. To do this we will help ourselves with a mixer, or whoever wants them can do so manually.

Batiremos un poco y luego, agregaremos un chorrito de la leche.  Batiremos de nuevo hasta mezclar todo, y entonces alternaremos con un hilo de aceite. Continuaremos el proceso batiendo y alternando ambos ingredientes, hasta que los dos se terminen y logremos una consistencia espesa.

The following will be to sedate our preparations. To this end, we will add a touch of dusted nut. We'll mix well and we'll test the fringes to check the date. If necessary, we will correct it with a touch of salt, and we will mix them back to fully integrate.
Acomoda la brandada en un recipiente para servir. Recupera los trocitos de ajo fritos para espolvorear la superficie del plato, y haz lo mismo con el perejil picado, si decidiste utilizarlo.
That's all. The cod frilling with slice of roasted bread can be accompanied.

traditional-cod-stew-teleculinaria.name: Traditional Cod Stew
instructions: Peel the potatoes, onions and garlic and cut them into slices, as well as peppers, previously cleaned from seeds. Book the potatoes in water. Remove skin and seeds from tomatoes and cut them into cubes. Also cut the cod posts into two or three pieces.

In a wide pot, put the ingredients in layers, starting with the onion, garlic, and blond leaves. Then add the potatoes, cod, peppers, and chilli cut into slices (without seeds). Season with wine, olive oil, tomato previously mixed with concentrate, salt and pepper.

Add the parsley and thyme branches, take them to the fire and let them cook for 25 minutes. As soon as it boils, lower the heat and let the cooking finish, stirring the pot from time to time. Remove from the fire and serve immediately.

traditional-coimbra-rice-pudding-telecul.name: Traditional Coimbra Rice Pudding
instructions: Boil the milk with the lemon peels. In the meantime, bring a pot to the fire with the water and when it is boiling add the rice. Let it cook for 4 minutes.
Add the hot milk (without lemon peels), slowly, letting it cook for another 35 minutes, in mild heat and always stirring.
Add sugar and let it boil again. Lie on a platter, let it cool and then serve, sprinkled with cinnamon powder.

traditional-colombian-christmas-fritters.name: Traditional Colombian Christmas Fritters

ingredients.item: 200 g of yuca starch (you can buy here)
250 g of grated coastal cheese, or blend of fresh and Feta cheese (you can buy here)
Opcional: una pizca de bicarbonato (ayuda a esponjar)

instructions: To start with, mix into a big bowl the 250 g of yucca starch, the 250 g of frosted coastal cheese, the 2 tablespoons of sugar, the teaspoon of salt and the teaspoon of chemical yeast.
In another bowl beat the egg and add it to the dry ingredients.

He starts integrating with his hands and sees his milk put up with him and put his milk up and down and down and down till you get a smooth and smooth mass. It should be tender, nonsticky and capable of making smooth balls. If you see that it looks dry, add an extra chorrit of milk and if you have passed, a little more starch and balance soon.

When the dough is ready, it forms balls the size of an appendage. Be sure to get them round and out of frying, because that's quite an influence on how good they are and how they don't get to frying them.

Put a pot or deep paddle onto the fire with enough oil to get the fringes floating. Warm at a medium-high temperature but not at best. A simple trick to check if it's ready is to put a small ball of dough: if it goes down and goes up slowly, it's perfect. If you come up at once, put the fire down a little.

Fry fringes in small tands so they don't pile up. You'll see that as they cook, they get inflamed and they start to get quite even. Turn them every once in a while with a mocha. The process usually takes a few minutes and as soon as you see them from a pretty gilt, get them out and let them run onto paper.
Let them rest for a second and... you have them. Crunching outside, soft inside and with that characteristic salty spot.

traditional-colombian-cold-cuts-comedera.name: Traditional Colombian Cold Cuts
instructions: The onion and garlic and sodas will be taken and the ground flesh added and the salt and pepper will be sewed. Cook her till she reduces all the juice.
At the same frying pan, pour the choricas till they are doradito and clear out all the frying.
Take the choricas and get cold.
Cut the bananas into frying and cold. Take the potatoes and get them and make them a purée.
Similarly, fry the eggs but have the yolk a little soft, if you don't prefer them that way, cook them completely.
Clean the banana leaves with a moist cloth.

traditional-couscous-or-tabbouleh-salad-.name: Traditional Couscous Or Tabbouleh Salad, Recipe
traditional-couscous-or-tabbouleh-salad-.original_name: Ensalada de cuscús tradicional o tabulé, receta fácil

ingredients.item: 4 hojas de menta o hierbabuena fresca

instructions: We're gonna start by cooking the cuscus. That way, once I'm ready, we can get him cold. To this end, we will heat up a cup and a half of water, that's the same amount of couscus we'll use (we can actually figure out an additional finger, just in case it evapates while warming it).
When the water's hot, add a touch of salt and pepper and an olive oil chorrit. Fold well.
Seat the cuscus into a bowl and pour hot water onto top. Cover and let the cuscus absorb the liquid.
Wait three minutes and then put the spoonful butter onto the hot cuscus. Cover to concentrate the heat and wait a few minutes for its frying.
Fold again after this time and mix with the help of a spoon or kitchen spatula. Then let the cuscus cool (clear) while we move to care about other ingredients.
Ahora vamos a lavar bien el perejil. Lo enjuagaremos bien con agua fresca, e incluso podemos dejarlo remojando en un recipiente con agua y un chorro de vinagre, durante unos cinco minutos.
Luego, vamos a escurrirlo muy bien, y con un cuchillo bien afilado, vamos a picarlo muy finamente sobre una tabla de cocina. Entonces, lo reservaremos.

We'll come to care about our other scented weed: mint or peppermint. We will also wash the leaves well (I would rather soak them with vinegar water as well as parsley) and then scold them and mingle them.
En el caso del tomate rojo, lo lavaremos bien y lo picaremos en cuadritos.
We will also peel the onion or onions, and we will mince them very finely.

Once the cuscus has been cold, we will start to mix the salad ingredients. We'll put all the vegetables in a salad and add the cuscus as well. Can you help with a fork to separate the grains from the latter.
Mezcla bien todos los ingredientes.

Then she pits a few lemons and squeezes her juice onto the salad (but removes the seeds). Add an additional touch of salt and pepper and an olive oil chorrit. Mix everything and try the tabular. Decide then if you want to squeeze the third lemon to suit the salad (the necessary amount of lemons may vary depending on their size). It also fixes the date, if necessary, with a little more salt and / or pepper. In any case, mix back very well.
Instead, let the salad refrigerate before serving it, for a more refreshing effect.

traditional-cream-milk-teleculinaria.name: Traditional Cream Milk
instructions: Light the milk with 1/3 of the sugar, the vanilla pod cut in half and shaved and let it boil.

In a separate bowl mix the remaining sugar with the moreena flour, add the yolks and stir well. Then pour the milk in yarn, stirring very well. Lie down for a pot and take it to the fire, always stirring until it thickens. Take it out of the fire and divide it in pieces. Sprinkle with some sugar, burn to taste and serve.

traditional-cuban-rice-with-egg-and-frie.name: Traditional Cuban Rice With Egg And Fried Banana
traditional-cuban-rice-with-egg-and-frie.original_name: Arroz a la cubana tradicional con huevo y plátano frito
instructions: First, cook the rice. In a saucepan, add 2 cups of rice and 4 cups of water (ratio usually 1: 2). Add a pint of salt and cook with medium-high heat till water starts to boil. Once it boils, reduce fire to a minimum, cover the saucer and let the rice cook till all water is absorbed and the rice is tender about 20 minutes.

While the rice's cooking, cook the tomato sauce. If you're using natural tomato, pits onion and garlic finely. In a frying pan, heat up some olive oil and add onion and garlic. Fry till onion's clear, then add the crushed tomato and a pint of salt. Let them cook at low heat for about 10-15 minutes, stirring from time to time.

While the salsa is cooked, pits the bananas and cut them into halves along. In another frying pan, heat butter with medium heat and fry the bananas till they are golden on both sides. Remove them and place them on a plate with absorbent paper.

In the same frying pan where you fry the bananas, add some olive oil and fry the eggs. Ideally, clear will be well cooked and still clear, as by breaking it will be mixed with rice, contributing creaminess to the plate.

Finally, it serves rice in individual plates and adds a fried banana and an egg. Cover the rice with some tomato sauce, and if you prefer, you can add some minced parsley or mint to give them a fresh touch.

traditional-cupcakes-teleculinaria.name: Traditional Cupcakes
instructions: Garnish for muffins with butter and sprinkle them with flour. Start the oven at 180 degrees.

In a bowl, beat the butter well with the sugar and some lemon zest. Then add the eggs, one by one and always beating very well. Add warm milk, always beating. Then add the flour, previously mixed with the yeast, and beat again.
Divide the previous mixture through the tins and put it in the oven for 25 minutes. Make sure the cupcakes are well cooked, remove them from the oven, unfurl them, let them cool and serve.

traditional-cuttlefish-stew-recipe-telec.name: Traditional Cuttlefish Stew Recipe
traditional-cuttlefish-stew-recipe-telec.original_name: Receita tradicional de ensopado de chocos
instructions: Season the rattles with salt and pepper and reserve. In a pot with olive oil, saute the onions, cut into half-moons, and garlic well chopped. Add the blonde and cook until the onion becomes translucent. Add the tomato pulp and let cook some more.
Add the shocks, water with the white wine and let cook, in mild heat, for 35 minutes, or until the rattles are well cooked. If necessary, add some water.
Cut the bread into thick slices and torre them. Retrieve the salt and pepper chops and put them on a plate. Spitter with chopped coriander and serve with the slices of toasted bread.

traditional-drowned-cakes-from-guadalaja.name: Traditional Drowned Cakes From Guadalajara
instructions: Now with clear chilies, we'll get them through a hot griddle and get them killed. We'll rotate them so they'll cook as evenly as possible, throughout their surface. We'll put them back later.
We will also wash well the tomato and then cook them for about 15 minutes, with low fire.

Mientras tanto, pelaremos la cebolla y la cortaremos en grandes trozos irregulares (solamente para facilitar el trabajo de la licuadora o robot de cocina que utilizaremos para procesarla posteriormente).
We'll also peel our garlic teeth.
Then we'll get two of his garlic teeth to a blender with half of his onion cuts, his pits drained, half a cup of cooking water, a teaspoon of salt, another of ground oregan, half of cumin and nails.

We'll process all the ingredients well in the blender, till we get a salsa as homogenous as we can. We will try and if necessary we will correct the date with more salt or spices. If you wish, you can add a sugar point.
Luego, reservaremos la salsa.

Prepararemos otra salsa con la otra mitad de la cebolla, los dos dientes de ajo restantes, los chiles asados, el vinagre y un toque de sal al gusto. Simplemente, procesaremos todos los ingredientes en la licuadora o robot de cocina.
After getting a uniform mixture, try the sauce and fix the date if necessary.
Reservaremos esta segunda salsa.
Calentaremos las carnitas y los frijoles y luego, abriremos los bolillos por la mitad.
En un plato grande, dejaremos a la mano la salsa de jitomate. El plato debe ser lo suficientemente grande como para sumergir las tortas.
Untaremos los panes (al centro) con un poco de frijoles refritos. Luego, dispondremos un poco de carnitas como relleno, y cerraremos cada torta.
Later, we will quickly sink every cake into the bowl with tomato sauce and after we get it out we will bath with some chili sauce.
Finalmente, rociaremos con un poco del jugo de los limones (previamente cortados en cuartos).
Puedes decorar tus tortas ahogadas con una juliana de cebollas o con cebollas encurtidas.

traditional-easter-bagel-recipe-comedera.name: Traditional Easter Bagel Recipe
traditional-easter-bagel-recipe-comedera.original_name: Receta de rosca de Pascua tradicional
instructions: Agrega 50 ml. agua y 13 gr. de levadura en un bowl grande y mezcla.
Add 2 eggs, 1 tablespoon of orange shell, 80 gr.of sugar, 250 gr. of force flour, 40 ml. olive oil at the above mix.
Revuelve todos los ingredientes hasta conseguir una masa consistente y uniforme.
Tapa y deja reposar por 20 minutos.
Vuelva la masa en una mesa con harina, amasa y da forma de circulo estilo doughnut.
Coloca un huevo cocido en el medio. Tapar y deja fermentar por 2 horas.
Una vez pase el tiempo, pinta con huevo toda la superficie.
Decorate with sugar noodles and borda for 20 minutes at 180 ″.
Deja reposar y disfruta junto a tu familia.

traditional-easter-torrijas-comedera.name: Traditional Easter Torrijas

ingredients.item: 1   vaina de vainilla o una cucharadita de extracto
1 / 2 kg hard bread (also with fresh bread)

instructions: How are calves prepared?

We'll start by washing the lemon well and then scrape the shell. It purports to use only the green and yellow part, and to scrape about nothing from the white part under the skin, because it has a bitter taste. Reserve the frying and use the rest of the lemon for another recipe, or to make juice.
If you have a vanilla pod, you have to open it along to remove the grains from the inside with the tip of a knife. If you're using the liquid extract, we'll miss this step.

Then we'll heat up the milk. When we start making bubbles, we'll add his vanilla, his sugar, his lemon and his cinnamon branch. We will revolve everything (by checking that the sugar will dissolve), we will let them start boiling and then we will remove milk from fire.
We're gonna cover the milk so it doesn't evaporate while we let it stand and cool. In the meantime, we can use them to take care of the bread.
With a close knife, we will cut the bread into slices about 2 and a half cm thick.

Then we will get a plate with edges or an equivalent container, where we can accommodate the cuts of bread, one next to each other. The next thing we have to do, we'll have them washed with scented milk (after we have removed the cinnamon branch). That's why it's important that the container have edges or walls, so that the liquid doesn't escape.

We'll give the bread a few minutes to get his milk impregnated. If necessary, we will turn the slices to absorb the liquid from both sides. We'll let the bread get soft (if it's about hard bread) but not too bad, so that it doesn't get rid of while cooking. If you use fresh bread, this stage will be much faster.
While the bread gets impregnated with milk, we'll add the contents of the eggs into a bowl. We'll beat him up and leave him at his hand and bounce his pieces of bread just before we fry them.

Then we'll warm up a big nonstick frying pan with oil or butter. We'll have a loaf of bread, we'll put it through the egg (both sides and cover it up) and then we'll fry it first for one face and then for the other. We'll let her get golden before we get her out of the cooking.
We'll repeat the same process till we cook the last calves.
Ideally we put them out of frying pan and put them onto a plate covered with an absorbing paper sheet. That way, we can remove the excess fat.
To end, sprinkle the calves with more cinnamon before serving them. Some prefer to sprinkle with additional sugar and cinnamon.

traditional-eggnog.name: Traditional Eggnog
instructions: Beat eggs with an electric mixer on medium speed until very frothy, 2 to 3 minutes.
Gradually beat in sugar, vanilla extract, and 1/4 teaspoon nutmeg.
Stir in cream, milk, brandy, and rum.
Cover and chill before serving. Sprinkle individual servings with remaining nutmeg.

traditional-eggplant-pie-argiro.name: Traditional Eggplant Pie
instructions: Mix all the ingredients together and add the water a little bit until the dough becomes firm.
Form on a stick and cut it into 8 pieces.
Place them in a floured bowl, to rest.

traditional-feijoada-teleculinaria.name: Traditional Feijoada

ingredients.item: 3 mature tomatoes (or 1 can tomato in pieces)

instructions: Season the ribs, bacon and ear with salt. Bring to the fire a pot with abundant water and salt. When it starts to boil, add the meats, the sausages and the thyme stem. Remove the meats as they get cooked.

Descasque e pique a cebola e os alhos. Elimine a pele e as sementes aos tomates e corte-os em cubinhos. Descasque a cenoura e corte-a em meias-luas ou em pedacinhos. Leve ao lume um tacho com o azeite, a cebola, os alhos e o louro. Deixe alourar um pouco e depois junte o tomate e a cenoura.
Add part of the water from the cooking of the meat and let it lift boiling. Add the cabbage cut into pieces, beans, tomato pulp and a little more salt and spicy sauce.

On the other hand, it bakes the rice in the remaining meat water, adding more water if necessary. Retrieve the seasonings and book. To the bean pot, add the meat cut into pieces and the sausages in slices, wrap and let it be discovered. Serve feijoada sprinkled with chopped parsley and accompany with rice.

traditional-feijoada-recipe-teleculinari.name: Traditional Feijoada Recipe
instructions: Pass the meats through water and cook them in salt-tempered water, along with the sausages. Remove from the water as they get cooked, let it cool and cut into pieces.
Descasque e pique finamente a cebola e os alhos; corte o repolho em tiras; escalde os tomates, retire-lhes a pele e as sementes e corte em cubos.

Light a pot with the olive oil, add the garlic and onion and let it saute. Add the tomato and blond and season with salt and pepper. Add the meats and cabbage and cover with some meat broth. Let it cook until the cabbage is soft.
Then add the beans and cut sausages and let it be clear. Retrieve the salt and serve hot.

traditional-filipino-lumpia.name: Traditional Filipino Lumpia
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon vegetable oil in a wok or large skillet over high heat. Add pork; cook and stir until crumbly and no longer pink, 5 to 7 minutes. Remove pork from the pan and set aside. Drain grease from the pan, leaving just a thin coating.
Add onion and garlic to the pan; cook and stir until fragrant, about 2 minutes.

Stir in cooked pork, carrots, green onions, cabbage, and cilantro. Season with pepper, salt, garlic powder, and soy sauce. Remove from the heat, and set aside until cool enough to handle, about 5 minutes.
To assemble lumpia: Place 3 heaping tablespoons of filling diagonally near one corner of a lumpia wrapper, leaving a 1 ½ inch space at both ends.
Fold the side along the length of the filling over the filling, tuck in both ends, and roll neatly and tightly to close.
Moisten the other side of the wrapper with water to seal the edge. Transfer to a plate and cover with plastic wrap to retain moisture. Repeat to assemble remaining lumpia.
Heat 1/2 inch vegetable oil in a heavy skillet over medium heat for 5 minutes.

Slide 3 to 4 lumpia into the hot oil, making sure the seams are facing down. Fry, turning occasionally, until all sides are golden brown, 1 to 2 minutes. Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate to drain. Repeat to fry remaining lumpia.
Serve immediately. Enjoy!

traditional-french-toast-teleculinaria.name: Traditional French Toast

ingredients.item: 8 slices (with 2 cm thick) bread cum or bread form with 3 days

instructions: Light half the milk with cinnamon stick, lemon peel and sugar and let it boil for 10 minutes in medium heat. Then add the remaining milk and discard the cinnamon stick and the lemon peels.
Pass the bread slices through the previous milk and then through the milked eggs. Fry them in hot oil on both sides until they're golden. Drain, sprinkle with cinnamon and reserve.
Leve ao lume um tacho com todos os ingredientes indicados para a calda e deixe ferver durante 3 minutos. Regue as rabanadas com esta calda, deixe arrefecer e sirva.

traditional-fricassee-teleculinaria.name: Traditional Fricassee
instructions: Cut the chicken into pieces and then peel and pinch the onions and garlic teeth. Lay the onion and garlic on a pot, add the butter and the laurel leaf and bring it to the fire, letting the onion become transparent.
Add the chicken pieces and stir. Add the chicken broth, season with some salt and pepper, cover and let cook, in mild heat, for 30 minutes.

In a bowl, add the lemon juice to the yolks and stir slightly. Add to the chicken pot, stir and boil, until it thickens. After some time, remove from the fire and serve sprinkled with chopped parsley, lemon slices and accompanied with white rice.

traditional-fried-rabbit-recipe-teleculi.name: Traditional Fried Rabbit Recipe
instructions: Peel the garlic teeth and pick them finely. Lay them down to a large bowl and add the pepper dough, white wine, laurel leaf, salt and pyri and mix. Add the rabbit pieces, stir well and let marinate in the cold overnight.
The next day, warm a frying pan with the olive oil, add the rabbit run and let it fry until it is brown on both sides. Remove the rabbit into a tray.

Add the marinade to the frying pan with the fat and let it boil well until the alcohol evaporates and the sauce clears. Water the rabbit with this sauce and bring it to the oven, preheated to 180oC for 10 minutes.
In the meantime, peel the potatoes, cut them into cubes, fry them in oil and drain. Remove the rabbit from the oven, transfer to a platter, dispose of the chips around, add pickles and serve.

traditional-gardener-teleculinaria.name: Traditional Gardener

ingredients.item: 400 g  carne de vaca para guisar

instructions: Descasque e pique a cebola e aloure-a num tacho com o azeite. Adicione o tomate e deixe refogar.
Corte a carne em cubos pequenos, adicione ao refogado, envolva e deixe cozinhar.
Peel and cut the carrots and potatoes into cubes. Add to the pot, as well as the green beans, previously arranged and cut into pieces.
Finally, add the peas and mint branch. Cover with water, lower the heat and let cook for about 20 minutes. In the end, correct the seasonings of salt and pepper and serve.

traditional-holy-week-torrijas-the-recip.name: Traditional Holy Week Torrijas (the Recipe)
traditional-holy-week-torrijas-the-recip.original_name: Torrijas tradicionales de Semana Santa (la MEJOR receta)

ingredients.item: 1 bar of bread from the previous day or brioche bread

instructions: Cortamos el pan en rodajas que disponemos en una fuente.
We put milk with cinnamon branch, 4 tablespoons of sugar and the skin of a lemon.
Cuando rompa a hervir retiramos del fuego y vertemos sobre el pan. Lo dejamos unos minutos para que el pan absorba toda la leche y se empape bien.
We put our balls in a bowl. We put a frying pan with virgin olive oil in the fire. When the oil's hot we put the bread through the egg.
We fry in the oil till they're golden. We get and put onto a plate with cooking paper to absorb the excess oil.
Then, still hot, we bounce into a mixture of sugar and cinnamon.

traditional-hot-or-cold-zucchini-cream-p.name: Traditional Hot Or Cold Zucchini Cream
traditional-hot-or-cold-zucchini-cream-p.original_name: Crema de calabacín tradicional caliente o fría

ingredients.item: 1 pastilla de caldo de verdura o 600 ml de caldo de verduras
2 cucharadas de aceite de oliva virgen extra (30 ml)

instructions: We wash and cut into cuts the 4 pumpkins (we can leave the skin if we want more color and nutrients).
Pelamos y troceamos la patata y 1oo g cebolla aproximadamente.
In a pint, we heated up 2 tablespoons of olive oil and revamed onion and pumpkin for 5 minutes with medium-low heat. Then we add the potato and we give it some laps as well.
We add 600 ml of vegetable or water broth with 1 pail and cook 20 minutes till the vegetables are tender.
Retiramos un poco de caldo (para ajustar la textura) y trituramos hasta obtener una crema fina.
Incorporamos 100 ml de nata y una pizca de nuez moscada, mezclamos y rectificamos de sal.
We serve hot or cold with fresh parsley and picket if we like.

traditional-italian-focaccia-recipe-come.name: Traditional Italian Focaccia Recipe

ingredients.item: 3/4  de kilo de harina de trigo para hacer pan
Aceite de oliva adicional para hornear

instructions: To start with, let's mix the flour with a tablespoon of sugar, into a bowl. We will also add the yeast, a tablespoon of salt and mix well all the ingredients.

With all these mixed ingredients, we will place the powder so that a hole remains in the middle. That's where we'll pour water and olive oil. Then we'll integrate the liquid with the powder, using our hands to form a mass. To this end, we will be making wrapped movements. The aim is to get a smooth and compact mix.
Luego pasaremos esta mezcla a una superficie plana, para amasarla. Antes de sacar la mezcla del bol, cubriremos la superficie con un poco de harina para que la masa no se pegue.
Bend up with a smooth and flexible consistency. That's usually about 10 to 15 minutes. Optional, we can cover our hands with oil or flour, to prevent the mass from hitting us.
Cuando la masa tenga un buen punto de elasticidad, cubriremos el bol con un trapo o toalla, y la dejaremos reposar una hora en un lugar fresco y seco.
Unos 15 minutos antes de que se termine el tiempo de reposo, vamos a precalentar el horno a 250 grados.

When the rest time of the mass expires, she should have doubled its size. Then we'll get it amassed quickly and then we'll grease a big Pyrex-type mold, or a plate or baking plate. That's where we'll put the dough and put them up till they have an even thickness. The ideal stage is to get a mass of 2 or 3 cm thick.

To purge the dough, we will wash the fresh rosemary and scrape and separate or mingle them into small pieces or branches. Ideally we complete a cup of rosemary and then we will sprinkle them about the focus and sink them with the help of our fingers.
To give the characteristic form of the focus, we will make some kind of holes in the mass with our fingers, without passing through or piercing them. We'll get them to work throughout the mass.
Finalmente, espolvorearemos con sal gorda y cubriremos con un hilo de aceite de oliva, que iremos distribuyendo por toda la superficie de la foccacia.
Hornearemos durante 25 minutos.
Once the cooking time's up, we'll get our focus out and cut them into pieces to serve them. That bread can be consumed hot or warm. I like it even when it's cold.

traditional-italian-limoncello.name: Traditional Italian Limoncello
instructions: Zest lemons, making sure to get the outermost part of the skin and avoid the white pith.
Place lemon zest in a glass bowl and cover with alcohol. Cover and let infuse at room temperature until yellowish in color, about 7 days.
Bring water and sugar to the boil in a saucepan, stirring until sugar dissolves. Remove syrup from heat and let cool, about 10 minutes.
Pour alcohol into the syrup through a fine-mesh strainer; discard lemon zest. Stir well.
Pour limoncello into thick, freezer-safe glass bottles. Store in the freezer for at least 2 weeks before serving.

traditional-italian-mushroom-risotto-peq.name: Traditional Italian Mushroom Risotto

ingredients.item: 250 g of fresh mushrooms, clear and cut into sheets
1 litro de caldo de verduras o de pollo caliente

instructions: Before you start cooking, make sure you have all the ingredients ready and ready. Wash and cut the mushrooms, paint the onion and garlic, and heat the vegetable or chicken stock into a saucer.

In a large frying pan, heat up some olive oil and add the chopped onion. Cooking with medium heat till clear, then add garlic and cook for another minute to let go of its smell. Add mushrooms to frying pan and cook till they are golden and have released their juices. That can take about 5- 7 minutes. Remove some mushrooms from the frying pan and save them to decorate the plate at the end.

Add arboric rice to frying pan and mix well with mushrooms and sodhyrit. Cooking for a few minutes till the rice's slightly golden. Fold the white wine into the frying pan and remove constantly until completely absorbed. It starts to add hot broth to frying pan, pint per pint while constantly stirring the rice. Wait till every tablespoon of broth has been absorbed before adding the next. That process can take about 18-20 minutes.

When the rice is at the tidal and has absorbed most of the stock, remove the frying pan from the fire and add butter and parmesan cheese. It removes vigorously till the butter has been melted and the cheese has been melted, creating that so desired creamy texture. Test risotto and title with salt and pepper if necessary. It serves hot, decorating with reserved mushrooms and some fresh parsley minced for a touch of color and freshness.

traditional-italian-porchetta-pequerecet.name: Traditional Italian Porchetta

ingredients.item: 2 kg de panceta de cerdo abierta en libro
1,2 kg de lomo de cerdo (para el relleno interior)
12 g de sal fina (una cucharada rasa)
8 g de pimienta negra molida (1 cucharadita colmada)
Cuerda de cocina suficiente para atar el rollo
Un chorrito extra de aceite de oliva para la superficie

instructions: The first thing to do is to prepare the adobo. In a large bowl we mix 1 tablespoon of salt, 1 tablespoon filled with black pepper, 4 minced garlic teeth, 1 tablespoon of granate ham, 1 tablespoon of fresh minced rosemary, 1 tablespoon of minced sage, 1 tablespoon of fraked lemon skin, 40 million extra virgin olive oil and 80 million dry white wine. It's got to be quite moist and easy to extend.

We place the pork pantet with the skin back onto the table. We smash her with our hands so that she's more uniform and we share the dough of the adobo throughout the surface and get well at the edges.
On top we put the 1,2 kg loin, that will play core, and we press a little to get it settled.
Now play roll. We start from the long side and we form a tight cylinder without a rush, making sure that the back remains focused.

Once we have him, we tie him with a kitchen string every 3-4 cm, as with a traditional Roast. There's no trick here: the better tied up, the more uniform the cooking and the more beautiful it will be at cutting.

Once we have the porchetta tied up, we cover it with paper aluminum, sealing the edges well so that steam remains inside. That helps a great deal: the flesh gets tender without drying and all the fat melts slowly, and that's what we want.

We put it into the oven at 150 ° C for 3 hours with the bottom fluid (a mixture of 200 ml of white wine and 150 ml of water) to maintain moisture. If during cooking we see that the bottom remains dry, we add some more water.

After that time, we carefully remove the aluminium (a steam that burns out, so care), we put up the oven at 200ºC and let it get dark from 25 to 30 minutes, till the skin gets crispy. Each oven is a world, so here, let's be guided by the light: when the surface has that golden tone and crosses by touching it, you'll know it's ready.

Once out of the oven, we let her rest at least 20- 30 minutes. It's an important step because the flesh sits and then cuts far better. If we try to cut it just out of the oven, it crumbles a little and lose juices.

traditional-kefalonia-almond-pie-argiro.name: Traditional Kefalonia Almond Pie

ingredients.item: 14 tsp. almond with rind chopped (no powder)
14 tsp. almond with rind chopped (no powder)

instructions: Mix the almond and toast in a bowl and keep them aside.
In a bowl, beat the yolks with sugar and vanilla for a few minutes until white.
Dissolve the baking powder into the liqueur and add them to the mixture.
With a clean mixer and a clean bowl, beat the whites, with a pinch of salt, in meringue.
We alternately shoot the beaten yolks, the meringue, the toast and the almond. Add the remaining meringue and fold the mixture.
Put it in a buttered baking pan of 30 cm.
Bake in a well preheated oven at 170°C in the resistance for about 1 hour, on the bottom grill.
Prepare the syrup by boiling the ingredients for 5′.
Let it get lukewarm.
Squeeze the hot sweet slowly with the lukewarm syrup.
Let it cool completely and then cut.

traditional-key-lime-pie.name: Traditional Key Lime Pie
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C).

Whisk condensed milk and egg yolks together in a large mixing bowl. Mix in Key lime juice; mixture will be thin at first but will thicken as it is whisked. Whisk until smooth and thick. Pour into the graham cracker crust.
Bake in the preheated oven until filling is firm to the touch and only slightly jiggly in the center, 12 to 15 minutes.
Cool to room temperature, then refrigerate before serving, 5 hours to overnight.

traditional-king-cake-teleculinaria.name: Traditional King Cake
instructions: Para preparar este bolo rei, pique a fruta cristalizada, coloque-a numa tigela, regue com o rum e deixe macerar.
Dissolve the yeast in warm water, add 100 g of flour, wrap well and let it leave for 15 to 20 minutes.

Beat the margarine with the sugar and the lemon and orange scrapes. Then add the eggs one by one and connect well between each addition. Add the dough with the yeast and turn it back on. Finally, add the remaining flour and salt and work the dough until it becomes soft and elastic.
Mix the fruit with the rum and work the dough until everything is well involved. Cover with a damp cloth and let it leave in a warm place for 2 hours and 30 minutes.

After this time, mold the cake-king-shaped dough and let it leave for another 30 minutes. Decorate with the dried fruits, brush with beaten egg and light in the oven, preheated at 180oC, for 40 minutes. Serve sprinkled with powdered sugar.

traditional-king-pie-argiro.name: Traditional King Pie

ingredients.item: 1/2 kg of flour inflated by itself
1 cup of almonds, roasted, coarsely chopped
1/2 kg of flour inflated by itself
1 cup of almonds, roasted, coarsely chopped

instructions: Shake the flour first with the baking powder.
Beat the butter and sugar until white and fluffy.
Add the egg yolks one at a time and then add the zest, machete, vanilla, milk and cognac.
In a bowl, add the salt whites.
Beat egg whites on tight meringue.
Add the flour and meringue to the mixture of the yolks, gently folding with a spatula.
Finally, add the coarsely chopped almonds and mix lightly.
Brush with some butter a baking pan No. 30 and add non-stick paper.
Pour the mixture into the baking pan and gently beat the air out.
Add the flurry for the royal pie.
If we want, sprinkle the basil pie with almond fillet.
Bake in a preheated oven at 170°C in the under grill resistance for about 55 minutes.
Allow it to stand for 10 minutes on the baking pan and turn it over on a rack to cool.
We sift upstairs, too, if we want, icing sugar.
Fluffy and delicious will bring luck to your home and many happy smiles to your loved ones. Fix it this year and you'll remember me!

traditional-lasagna-from-astypalaia-argi.name: Traditional Lasagna From Astypalaia

ingredients.item: 800 gr of green (flower or fresh myzithra)
800 gr of green (flower or fresh myzithra)

instructions: For the handmade stuffed lasagna, in a bowl, mix first all the liquid ingredients for the dough and slowly add the flour until it becomes a hard dough.
In a briquet, dry the saffron (or saphor). Beat it in the mortar to powder and dissolve it in a little hot water. Add salt and pepper.
Open thin leaf and fill in the shape of caramel.
Boil in salted water until they rise to the surface.
Strain and pour with hot butter.
Thank you very much for sharing this traditional recipe from Astypalea.

traditional-malaga-cod-and-orange-salad-.name: Traditional Malaga Cod And Orange Salad
traditional-malaga-cod-and-orange-salad-.original_name: Ensalada malagueña tradicional de bacalao y naranja
instructions: If we haven't been with them before, we have to get rid of the cod. To achieve this we put cod into cold water throughout the night, if it's demayed or for 24 hours with one or two water changes for steaks. We have to do this by bringing cod into the refrigerator.

To cook the potatoes we wash them and put them in cold water with salt. Once they start to boil them we will have to hold them for 20- 30 minutes, depending on the size of the potatoes. To figure out if they're already tender, we put them with a sharp knife and they have the right texture.
Once they cook the potatoes with skin, we let them warm up, we peel and cut them into pieces. In our case we have been with thick cuts, but you can also cut them into big pieces if you prefer.

The orange we peel "live," that's to say, eliminating any white parts that have built up, as it can get bitter. The orange can be cut into shackles or thick cuts, depending on how you wish to put up the salad.
Para la vinagreta, picamos algunas aceitunas y emulsionamos con aceite de oliva virgen extra, sal y vinagre.

To ride the salad with cod, we put first a cape of potatoes and we spit. We put up an egg and an onion or a tanked onion. We place despelled and demayed cod into pieces. We finally have pawned oranges and olives, which are traditionally alorean olives typical of Málaga but can be replaced by any green olive or black olives. We finally have pawned oranges and olives, which are traditionally alorean olives typical of Málaga but can be replaced by any green olive or black olives. We scold with the vinegar, mix well and serve.

traditional-manchego-ratatouille-grandmo.name: Traditional Manchego Ratatouille, Grandmother's Recipe
traditional-manchego-ratatouille-grandmo.original_name: Pisto manchego tradicional, la receta casera de la abuela
instructions: We put extra virgin olive oil and add garlic and onion.
Rehogamos unos minutos e incorporamos los pimientos verdes.
We had a few minutes and added pumpkins, without peeling and also cut into pits.
We leave another few minutes and finally we add the shredded tomato or grated tomato, some salt, fresh ground black pepper and ground cumin.
We cook everything with soft heat till the tomato's fried.

traditional-manestra-of-santorini-argiro.name: Traditional Manestra Of Santorini

ingredients.item: 3 tablespoons of kefalotyri or sandorine lard
3 tablespoons of kefalotyri or sandorine lard

instructions: For this recipe ideally we use arid Santorini tomatoes, alternatively 600 gr finely chopped or crushed tomatoes.
Wash and grind the tomatoes in the blender.
Place them in a pot and let them boil for 10′ in medium heat.
They should almost dry up and take out all their perfume.
Add the olive oil and bake the tomato for 5’.
Add 1 litre of hot water and boil for 10’, add the broken pasta.
Boil for 3’-4’ stirring once or twice.
Lower the heat to cut the boil, and pour the eggs into the manestra with care, without mixing them.
We want them to stay whole and boil without breaking up, boil them for 3’.
Add the grated cheese and some pepper. We're checking if he needs salt.
Turn off the fire and let the food stand for 5’
Serve the juicy red manestra in deep dishes and add 1 or 2 eggs to each portion.

traditional-mango-mousse-comedera.name: Traditional Mango Mousse

ingredients.item: 1  mango grande muy maduro (aproximadamente 300 g de pulpa)
40 g of sugar (adjustable by mango sweeter)
4 gr de gelatina sin sabor (media cucharada)
2 cdas de agua para hidratar la gelatina
1 cda de yogur griego natural (opcional, aporta cuerpo y ligera acidez)

instructions: Peel the mango and cut the pulp into cubes. To process till you get a completely smooth purée. If you want a more thin texture, strain to remove fibers.
Add lemon juice, pint of salt and sugar to purée. Mix to fully integrate.
Hidratar la gelatina sin sabor con las dos cucharadas de agua durante 5 minutos. Luego llevarla al microondas por 10 segundos hasta que se disuelva sin hervir.
Incorporate dissolved Jelatin into mango purée while constantly mixing to avoid clumps.
Beat cold cream till you get soft peaks. It should not be too firm as it will lose air while integrating it.
Add Greek yogurt to mango purée (optional) and mix.
Incorporate the beamed cream with wrapped movements, using a spatula, to maintain the characteristic airy texture of the mousse.
Fold the mixture into single cups or small mold.
Refrigerate at least 2 hours till the mousse is firm and creamy.
Decorate with small cubes of fresh mango or a touch of lime fringes before serving.

traditional-maritime-hodge-podge.name: Traditional Maritime Hodge Podge
instructions: Cook bacon in a large, deep skillet over medium-high heat until evenly browned, about 10 minutes. Drain on a paper towel-lined plate, then crumble and set aside.

Meanwhile, melt 2 tablespoons butter in a large pot over medium heat. Stir in onion and garlic; cook and stir until garlic has softened and onion begins to turn golden brown, about 8 minutes. Scrape mixture into a small bowl. Remove garlic clove, mash, and return to the bowl.

Place beans and carrots into the same pot. Pour in chicken broth and enough water to just cover the vegetables; season with salt. Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 15 minutes.
Stir in potatoes and cook until tender, about 30 minutes. Stir in garlic-onion mixture, cream, and remaining 4 tablespoons butter; return to a simmer.
Dissolve flour in 1/2 cup water and stir into the soup. Cook and stir until soup has thickened, 10 to 15 minutes. Serve with crumbled bacon.

traditional-meat-lasagna-teleculinaria.name: Traditional Meat Lasagna

ingredients.item: 350 g tomato in pieces (without skin or grains)

instructions: Light a pot with water, oil and salt and let it boil. Add the lasagna leaves and let cook for 5 minutes. Then pass them cold water, dry them with absorbent paper and reserve.

Light a pot with half the olive oil and the laurel leaf and let it warm. Add the minced meat and let it cook, stirring until it is loose. Season with salt and pepper and stir. Remove from the flame, transfer it to a drainer and let it run.

On the other side, melt the butter, add the flour and, without stopping stirring, let it boil 1 or 2 minutes. Add the milk in yarn and keep stirring until it thickens. Season with salt, pepper and nutmeg, remove from the fire and reserve.

Bring another pot to the fire with the remaining oil, garlic and onions, chopped, and let it shine. Add the tomato, season with salt, sugar, nutmeg, pepper and oregano and stir. Add the meat, correct the seasonings and remove from the fire.

In a pyrox or refractory container (30x20 cm), make alternate layers of bechamel sauce, lasagna leaves and meat. The last layer should be bechamel sauce. Sprinkle with grated cheese and light oven, preheated to 180°C, until gratin.

traditional-meat-pie-gizlemopita-argiro.name: Traditional Meat Pie (gizlemopita)

ingredients.item: 1 kg of beef (or beef and lamb mix)
5 small leeks (or 2 large) tenderly in slices
1 kg of beef (or beef and lamb mix)
5 small leeks (or 2 large) tenderly in slices

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a wide and shallow pot.
Add the chopped meat or minced meat and brown it for 6-7 minutes to drink its liquids.
Add the chopped leeks and finely chopped onions. Leave them on medium heat to wilt for a few minutes, without color. From time to time, stir.
Salt and pepper and withdraw from the fire.
Add the petoura (traditional chlopites Kozani) and mix.
Allow the filling to cool for a few minutes.
Beat the eggs with milk or yogurt and add them to the filling by mixing.
If we want, add a little chopped parsley. Mix the filling.
In shallow, large baking pan for pie, 40 cm (or 35×28 cm long baking pan) apply some olive oil.
Apply three radial leaves , sprinkle them in between with a few drops of olive oil. Make sure the leaves are quite prominent from the baking pan.
Empty the filling and with a spoon spread it into a thin layer.
Cover with 1 sheet, slightly wrinkled. Drizzle with olive oil. Cover with the last leaf, slightly wrinkled.
Inwardly turn the edges that stand out on the pie and turn into a loose pit.
Add a large glass to the center of the pie and make a circle around it with a sharp knife. We do not carve down, but only the top leaves.
Radially, divide the pie into 12 pieces. Drizzle with the remaining olive oil and sprinkle the surface with some water.

Bake the pie in a well preheated oven at 180°C on the resistors, on the bottom grill, for about 1 hour, until the base of the baking pan is well browned. In the last 10 minutes we only bake with the lower resistance for a crisper result.
The pie is ready when it is well browned and completely removed from the baking pan.

traditional-meatballs-with-mashed-potato.name: Traditional Meatballs With Mashed Potatoes
traditional-meatballs-with-mashed-potato.original_name: Almôndegas tradicionais com puré de batata
instructions: Prepare the mashed potatoes: peel the potatoes, cut them in rooms, bake them in boiling water seasoned with salt, then drain them and reduce them to puree. Take it back to the light and add the milk until it reaches the desired consistency. Add the butter, salt, pepper and nutmeg, stir well, remove from the fire and reserve.

Peel the onions and garlic and pick them finely. In a bowl, mix the minced meat well with the eggs, flour, oregano, some onion and garlic, salt and pepper. Mold meatballs, with floury hands, and reserve.

Light a pot with the remaining onion and garlic, the blond and olive oil and let it shine. Add the chopped tomato, stir and, minutes later, add the tomato pulp, a pinch of salt and pepper and sugar. Let it cook a little, stirring from time to time.
Water with the wine, add the meatballs and cook in a light for 30 minutes, stirring from time to time. Serve the meatballs, watered with tomato sauce and accompanied with mashed potatoes.

traditional-mexican-chilorio-comedera.name: Traditional Mexican Chilorio

ingredients.item: 600 gr de carne de paleta de cerdo (en trozos)

instructions: We'll start by getting a big pot, in which we can cook meat without any space problems. That's where we'll put the meat, clear but without his disposal of the fat parts that I could eventually have. That will help to stay more hydrated.
To cook, we'll cover with a few cups of water and some salt to suit. If you wish, you can taste water with some oregano powder, a bay leaf and a peppermint (fresh, preferably).

We'll let the meat cook for 40 minutes. To this end, there are two schools. One, that cooks up the stuffed meat but keeps an close eye on her to make sure that she moves often and that there are no parts to cook outside the liquid, because this would resect her. The second school, cook the stuffed pork to be submerged all the time. That's up to the cook.
Take care of how long the pork takes to cook and do other jobs. Wash the chilies and remove their branch remains, seeds and veins.
It steals some water and when you remove it from the fire, use it to submerge the chilies. Just leave them there for 10 minutes, so they get soft.
Mientras tanto, pela los dientes de ajo.

After the time of mooring the chilies, scrape them and put them to a blender. Add garlic to them, half a teaspoon oregano powder coffee maker, half pony, a pint of salt, one of sugar and one of pepper.

He processes everything to get a salsa. If necessary, help with a little of the water you used to boil the chilies, so that the blender will tighten all the ingredients. Reserve this sauce for the moment.

When the meat's ready, heat up a big frying pan with some butter. Drain the pieces of meat and cook them quickly to get some light, onto the surface. We have to do this very quickly so that the pork doesn't get cold.
When you remove the meat from the frying pan, it proceeds with the help of a pair of forks.

Reforms the pot where you cook the pork earlier and removes any other fluids. If necessary, add a little more butter and then get back pork, washed with chili sauce prepared in the blender. Add 3 tablespoons of vinegar, mix everything and let them cook under low heat for 20 minutes. Don't cover up the pot.
After this cooking time, try the chilorium to check the date. If necessary, fix with more salt and pepper, or other spices, and then mix very well.

traditional-mexican-cream-gorditas-comed.name: Traditional Mexican Cream Gorditas

ingredients.item: 1 cucharadita cafetera de levadura o polvo para hornear
1 taza de crema de leche o nata

instructions: The first thing we'll do will be to mix the bake or bake powder with milk. We'll reserve the mix momentarily.
Then we'll mix with the sugar and the egg.
When well integrated, we will add milk cream, vanilla essence and flour.
Also add a pint of salt to the production.

It well integrates all the ingredients up to a homogenous mix. He then incorporates the milk we had previously booked. Do so little by little so that it will be mixed well and so as to avoid splashing with the liquid outside the container you are using.
Mix up and get an even dough. If necessary, continue to beat till the dough's soft.

Once the mass is ready, there are two "schools" to prepare the chordites. One would rather extend the dough to a relatively thin film, and then cut it into a chordite with a cookie cutter. Another person prefers to make balls with the dough, and then put them up to get the tights. I prefer the last method.
Cook the tights onto a hot paddle or plate (low heat!), slightly greased. You can also use a large frying pan if you don't have the other two options.
When the tights are golden on one face, turn them to cook on the other. Help yourself with a wooden scatula.
Once you have been golden, your tipples will be ready to eat. I recommend using them right away, because nothing but a hot cream tit. & lt; 3

traditional-mexican-esquites-comedera.name: Traditional Mexican Esquites

ingredients.item: 2 cucharadas de manteca (de origen animal o vegetal)

instructions: We'll start with peeling and minting into small cubes.
En el caso del chile, lo lavaremos y le cortaremos la cabeza. Luego, lo abriremos para retirar la vena y las semillas, y picaremos la pulpa muy finamente.
Wash up and spread them into several parts.
To continue, we will heat up a large frying pan (with medium heat) with the butter, till its frying.
When the butter's hot, we'll add the onion pieces to the frying pan and cook them till they start to become transparent.
Then we will add the already clear corn grains, the serrane chili (optional), the pieces of ebazote, a pint of salt and the chicken stock.
Mezclaremos bien los ingredientes y taparemos la olla, para dejarlos cocinarse durante 8 minutos.
After this time, we will check the consistency of elote grain, to see if it's ready or if necessary, let it cook a little more. We'll also check that. If necessary, we'll fix with a little more salt.

To serve, use cups or cups and fill them with grains and broth, helping with a spoonful of soup. Can water the elotes with lemon juice, or give a piece of lemon to every dinner party, to do this personally.
Put some mayonnaise onto the ski. Filling with broken white cheese with piquin chili powder and optionally with a little more salt.

traditional-mexican-stuffed-chili-with-t.name: Traditional Mexican Stuffed Chili, With Turkey Meat
traditional-mexican-stuffed-chili-with-t.original_name: Chile relleno mexicano tradicional, con carne de pavo

ingredients.item: 3.5 oz. zanahoria cortada en cubos chicos
1.5 oz. cebolla blanca cortada en juliana
Sal y pimienta molida a gusto

instructions: Let's get the red tomatoes roasted on a paddle or plate. Otherwise we can use a frying pan. The idea is to turn them around and roast them all their faces. We also peel garlic teeth and roast them as well as onion.

Con un cuchillo bien afilado, hacemos un corte lateral de la cabeza al extremo inferior de cada chile. Muy cuidadosamente, nos ayudaremos con el cuchillo para retirar las semillas del interior de cada chile, y la vena o parte blanca.

Then we also roasted the village chilies. We'll rotate them onto a hot plate or even directly onto the fire. The skin will possibly be pitted or blackened in some parts. Don't be afraid. That's exactly what we're trying to get.

We'll let the chilies rest about five minutes, locked up in a plastic bag, so they "sweat." Then we'll carefully remove the skin. The process we have previously undertaken will make this step easier. If necessary, you can pass them under a tibia jet to remove the last remaining pieces of skin.

In a hot frying pan, we will vertilate sunflower oil and apitra onion, garlic and carrot. Once ready, we add JENNIE-O ® Fur of Fold Turkey. When cooked, we add the tomato purée and mix all the ingredients. We spotted some cilantro pits and added them to the mix.
We filled the chilies with the JENNIE-O ® JENNIEEN Fur Fur.

Now we get the sauce ready. In a blender, we will process roasted tomatoes with or without skin (we suggest leave them skin), garlic, onion, parsley leaves (well washed), chicken stock and a touch of salt, pepper and oregan.
Once his motif's clear, we'll put him into a pot and keep him under fire. Test them to check and correct them if necessary.
When we get the chilies out of the oven, we'll serve them on a plate, with a little knapper. If we have used wood chopsticks to close them, we have to remove them before serving them.

traditional-mince-pies.name: Traditional Mince Pies
instructions: Preheat the oven to 204°C (200 degrees C).

Sift flour, 1/4 cup confectioners' sugar, and cinnamon together in a large bowl. Use a pastry cutter or two forks to mix in butter until mixture resembles fine crumbs. Stir in orange zest. Sprinkle with ice water and gather dough into a ball. Roll out on a lightly floured surface to 1/4 inch thick. Cut out approximately 18 (3-inch) diameter circles, and 18 (2-inch) circles, re-rolling dough as needed.

Line muffin cups or mini tart tins using the 3-inch pastry circles. Fill each pastry cup with about 1 tablespoon of mincemeat filling. Top with 2-inch pastry circles, sealing edges firmly together or crimping lightly with a fork to prevent the filling from leaking as they cook. Brush the top of each pie with egg.
Bake mince pies in preheated oven until tops are golden brown, 15 to 20 minutes. Cool slightly on wire racks. Dust with remaining 1/4 cup confectioners' sugar just before serving.

traditional-molotoff-teleculinaria.name: Traditional Molotoff
instructions: Lay the yolks on a pot, add half the sugar and water, mix and then take it to the fire, stirring until it thickens. Go through the hammock, lie in a bowl and let it cool.

Start the oven at 180oC, grease a hole with margarine and sprinkle it with sugar. In a bowl, beat the clears in a very firm castle, gathering the remaining 150 g of sugar gradually and always beating. Finally, add the liquid caramel and wrap.

Pour the previous preparation into the form, smooth and bake for 16 minutes. Remove, deform, put on a wide dish, let it cool, water with the gem candy that prepared and serve decorated with roasted rolled almond.

traditional-mushroom-pie-of-grevena-argi.name: Traditional Mushroom Pie Of Grevena
instructions: Spread two leaves on sheet and bake them at 180°C for 10 minutes, until dry and slightly browned.
Let them cool.
Melt the butter into a large pan, add some of the olive oil and add the mushrooms to a strong heat for 4-5 minutes, until they drink their liquids.
Add the onions and garlic and sauté until they wilt.
Add the tomato, salt, pepper and buffo.
Boil the filling for a few minutes until it drinks its liquids.
After it cools, add the walnut and the up and mix.
Break the baked leaves.
Brush a large baking pan with olive oil and spread two leaves, also rubbing them with olive oil.
Spread the broken baked leaves in, empty the filling and cover the pie with two more oiled intermediate leaves.
Add the pie, oil the surface well and sprinkle it with water.
Bake in a well preheated oven at 180°C, in the resistances for 1 hour, until the pie is well browned and unstuck.

traditional-mykonos-onion-pie-argiro.name: Traditional Mykonos Onion Pie

ingredients.item: 6 village leaves (or two traditional thick leaves)
1 kg of mycone firearms, (or fresh myzithra or anthrotyr or cheese mixture)
10 fresh onions (deep with their green leaves)
6 village leaves (or two traditional thick leaves)
1 kg of mycone firearms, (or fresh myzithra or anthrotyr or cheese mixture)
10 fresh onions (deep with their green leaves)

instructions: You can make your own peasant leaf.
They melt the cheeseball and the other cheeses with a fork in a bowl.
Add the herbs, salt and pepper and the broken pides.
We choose tenderly fresh onions.
If they're too big, use half.
We'll use their green leaves.
Finely chop the fresh and dry onions.
Add them to the cheese.
Beat the eggs with oil in a bowl and add them to the cheeses.
Mix the filling with a spoon.
If you have fresh fennel, you put it because it makes the pie magical.
Generally on the Aegean islands we use much fennel and fresh mint, especially on pies.
Otherwise, add a little more dill.
In an oily shallow baking pan of 40 million for pie, we lay three sheets radially oiling them in between.
Empty the filling and spread the ingredients with a spoon.
Turn the edges of the leaves that are prominent on the filling.
Cover the pie with the rest of the leaves.
Place them wrinkled and sprinkle olive oil between the leaves.
We drill the whole surface of the pie and make it surface.
Pour with the remaining olive oil and some water, sprinkle the surface.
Bake in a well preheated oven at 180 °C in the resistors (up and down) for about 1 hour, until the onion pie is browned up and down.
For a crisper result, I only bake for 20 minutes in the lower resistance.
I'm letting the pie stand for 15 minutes and draw its fluids. So the leaf will remain crispy until the last bite.

traditional-or-air-fryer-fried-cod-peque.name: Traditional Or Air Fryer Fried Cod
traditional-or-air-fryer-fried-cod-peque.original_name: Bacalao frito tradicional o en freidora de aire

ingredients.item: 500 g de bacalao desalado (en lomos o trozos)
1 egg (optional, if you want more consistent bouncing)
1 diente de ajo (opcional, para aromatizar el aceite)
Salt (only if cod isn't at its best)

instructions: The first thing we do is to make sure that cod's well despaired. If we have bought them ready to cook, we will save ourselves this step. Otherwise, we leave it in water in the refrigerator about 24 to 48 hours, changing water every 8 hours.
We dry up the pieces of cod with cooking paper. That step's key to how the flour goes well and doesn't get a frying oil.
We heat up abundant olive oil in a big frying pan. If we wish, we can add a peeled garlic tooth to give a taste, but then we remove it before frying.
We have been tipping up the cod from all sides. If we prefer a more crisp pounding, we can also pass it after the flour.

When the oil is hot but without smoking (about 170-180 ° C), we frying the pieces of cod by tands, without pounding them up. We turn them around while they're golden and we get them out while they're crispy and juicy and about 3 minutes and about depending on the thickness.
We let them slip onto paper to remove excess oil and we serve them freshly made with some parsley above them if we want to give them a touch of color.

traditional-orange-chicken-pequerecetas.name: Traditional Orange Chicken
instructions: Ponemos aceite de oliva virgen extra en la cacerola y doramos las piezas de pollo salpimentadas a fuego vivo. Una vez doradas, los sacamos y reservamos.
In the same oil we put to polish both garlic teeth and two minced onions. When they are spotted we add the juice from the oranges.
Con la batidora trituramos todo hasta que quede una salsa suave.
We have some fine tirites from the orange skin that you can do with an acanalador or with a paddle
We add the chicken thighs and let them cook with soft heat about 20 or 30 minutes.
We serve once the chicken's been cooked and the sauce's been reduced.

traditional-oriental-ajem-pilaf-argiro.name: Traditional Oriental Ajem Pilaf
instructions: Rinse the rice in a colander until the water looks transparent to leave all the starch.
Pour it with the lemon juice and let it drain well.
Heat the oil with the butter and break it into small pieces.
Allow it to rust for a few minutes.
Add the rice and sauté for a few minutes until the grain of rice looks clear and clear.
Add the onion and set it aside for 2-3′.
Add the broth, salt and pepper.
Lower the heat, half the pot and simmer for 20′ at the lower intensity.
When little holes appear on the surface it is ready.
Add 1 tablespoon butter, lightly mix and control salt and pepper.
Remove from heat, cover with clean towel and lid.
Let the traditional oriental ajem pilafi stand for 10 minutes.
Serve the ajem on a platter along with the kebabs, a lot of sauce and if you want to garnish with a garnished kefalotiri.

traditional-pancake-recipe-comedera.name: Traditional Pancake Recipe

instructions: Make dry rub: Combine brown sugar, paprika, mustard, sea salt, garlic powder, onion powder, and coriander in a small bowl; mix well.
Prepare ribs: Coat ribs all over with oil and sprinkle with dry rub. Set aside at room temperature for 1 hour.
Heat a charcoal or gas grill for indirect cooking to 230 to 121°C (110 to 120 degrees C). Add drained wood chips.

Place ribs, meaty-side down, over a drip pan and cook on the preheated grill for 2 hours, basting occasionally with sprays of apple juice over both sides of ribs. Add about 12 coals to a charcoal grill about every 45 minutes to maintain heat.

Remove ribs from the grill and double-wrap each rack in heavy-duty aluminum foil. Return wrapped ribs to the grill and cook over indirect heat until ribs are very tender, 1 hour 30 minutes to 3 hours. Add more coals as needed to maintain the temperature for a charcoal grill.

traditional-sauerkraut-with-caraway.name: Traditional Sauerkraut With Caraway

ingredients.item: Pounder (a heavy implement like the end of a rolling pin

ingredients.prep: a whole cabbage leaf or small plate

instructions: Weigh the shredded cabbage to ensure the salt to cabbage ratio is correct. The amount of salt you use should come to about 1.5–2.5%, but no more than 3%, of the cabbage weight.
In a large bowl, mix and massage the salt thoroughly through the cabbage, making sure to distribute the salt evenly.
Let the cabbage sit to sweat a bit, for about 10 minutes. This is simply to make the next step easier. Use this time to get your fermentation vessel cleaned.

With your pounder, pound quite energetically for about 5–10 minutes, until the cabbage is dripping with its own salty water when you pick up a handful. This liquid is your brine. (You can also use the dough hook of a stand mixer for a few minutes to speed things up, but do not let it run for too long.)
Next, mix in the caraway seeds (if using).
Put the mixture into the jar, packing it down tightly as you go using the pounder. Push down well, particularly at the end, to coax out any more brine. You need the brine to cover the cabbage.

Do not pack the cabbage all the way to the top; leave some headroom at the top of the jar to allow for a bit of growth and movement and, of course, the weight. You don’t want the liquid touching the top of the lid, as it will end up spewing out of your air-lock or up out of your lid.
Cover with a cabbage leaf (the follower), the weight, and then your chosen lid or system.

Depending on your ferment, you can start trying it as soon as you’d like, but the less you fiddle with it in the first 2 weeks, the better. It is ready when you think it is delicious. With the right system and temperature, you can leave it to ferment for months before refrigeration.

If you used a crock, you’ll need to decant the kraut to smaller jars before you refrigerate, unless you have a walk-in cool room or large cellar. It will keep in the fridge for 12 months or more. Use your senses to determine freshness.
